5193					if (!type_is_rowtype(valtype))
5194						ereport(ERROR,
5195								(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
5196								 errmsg("cannot assign non-composite value to a record variable")));
5197					exec_move_row_from_datum(estate, (PLpgSQL_variable *) rec,
5198											 value);
5199				}
5200				break;
5201			}
5202
5203		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_RECFIELD:
5204			{
5205				/*
5206				 * Target is a field of a record
5207				 */
5208				PLpgSQL_recfield *recfield = (PLpgSQL_recfield *) target;
5209				PLpgSQL_rec *rec;
5210				ExpandedRecordHeader *erh;
5211
5212				rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) (estate->datums[recfield->recparentno]);
5213				erh = rec->erh;
5214
5215				/*
5216				 * If record variable is NULL, instantiate it if it has a
5217				 * named composite type, else complain.  (This won't change
5218				 * the logical state of the record, but if we successfully
5219				 * assign below, the unassigned fields will all become NULLs.)
5220				 */
5221				if (erh == NULL)
5222				{
5223					instantiate_empty_record_variable(estate, rec);
5224					erh = rec->erh;
5225				}
5226
5227				/*
5228				 * Look up the field's properties if we have not already, or
5229				 * if the tuple descriptor ID changed since last time.
5230				 */
5231				if (unlikely(recfield->rectupledescid != erh->er_tupdesc_id))
5232				{
5233					if (!expanded_record_lookup_field(erh,
5234													  recfield->fieldname,
5235													  &recfield->finfo))
5236						ereport(ERROR,
5237								(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
5238								 errmsg("record \"%s\" has no field \"%s\"",
5239										rec->refname, recfield->fieldname)));
5240					recfield->rectupledescid = erh->er_tupdesc_id;
5241				}
5242
5243				/* We don't support assignments to system columns. */
5244				if (recfield->finfo.fnumber <= 0)
5245					ereport(ERROR,
5246							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
5247							 errmsg("cannot assign to system column \"%s\"",
5248									recfield->fieldname)));
5249
5250				/* Cast the new value to the right type, if needed. */
5251				value = exec_cast_value(estate,
5252										value,
5253										&isNull,
5254										valtype,
5255										valtypmod,
5256										recfield->finfo.ftypeid,
5257										recfield->finfo.ftypmod);
5258
5259				/* And assign it. */
5260				expanded_record_set_field(erh, recfield->finfo.fnumber,
5261										  value, isNull, !estate->atomic);
5262				break;
5263			}
5264
5265		default:
5266			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized dtype: %d", target->dtype);
5267	}
5268}
5269
5270/*
5271 * exec_eval_datum				Get current value of a PLpgSQL_datum
5272 *
5273 * The type oid, typmod, value in Datum format, and null flag are returned.
5274 *
5275 * At present this doesn't handle PLpgSQL_expr datums; that's not needed
5276 * because we never pass references to such datums to SPI.
5277 *
5278 * NOTE: the returned Datum points right at the stored value in the case of
5279 * pass-by-reference datatypes.  Generally callers should take care not to
5280 * modify the stored value.  Some callers intentionally manipulate variables
5281 * referenced by R/W expanded pointers, though; it is those callers'
5282 * responsibility that the results are semantically OK.
5283 *
5284 * In some cases we have to palloc a return value, and in such cases we put
5285 * it into the estate's eval_mcontext.
5286 */
5287static void
5288exec_eval_datum(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
5289				PLpgSQL_datum *datum,
5290				Oid *typeid,
5291				int32 *typetypmod,
5292				Datum *value,
5293				bool *isnull)
5294{
5295	MemoryContext oldcontext;
5296
5297	switch (datum->dtype)
5298	{
5299		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_PROMISE:
5300			/* fulfill promise if needed, then handle like regular var */
5301			plpgsql_fulfill_promise(estate, (PLpgSQL_var *) datum);
5302
5303			/* FALL THRU */
5304
5305		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR:
5306			{
5307				PLpgSQL_var *var = (PLpgSQL_var *) datum;
5308
5309				*typeid = var->datatype->typoid;
5310				*typetypmod = var->datatype->atttypmod;
5311				*value = var->value;
5312				*isnull = var->isnull;
5313				break;
5314			}
5315
5316		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_ROW:
5317			{
5318				PLpgSQL_row *row = (PLpgSQL_row *) datum;
5319				HeapTuple	tup;
5320
5321				/* We get here if there are multiple OUT parameters */
5322				if (!row->rowtupdesc)	/* should not happen */
5323					elog(ERROR, "row variable has no tupdesc");
5324				/* Make sure we have a valid type/typmod setting */
5325				BlessTupleDesc(row->rowtupdesc);
5326				oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
5327				tup = make_tuple_from_row(estate, row, row->rowtupdesc);
5328				if (tup == NULL)	/* should not happen */
5329					elog(ERROR, "row not compatible with its own tupdesc");
5330				*typeid = row->rowtupdesc->tdtypeid;
5331				*typetypmod = row->rowtupdesc->tdtypmod;
5332				*value = HeapTupleGetDatum(tup);
5333				*isnull = false;
5334				MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
5335				break;
5336			}
5337
5338		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC:
5339			{
5340				PLpgSQL_rec *rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) datum;
5341
5342				if (rec->erh == NULL)
5343				{
5344					/* Treat uninstantiated record as a simple NULL */
5345					*value = (Datum) 0;
5346					*isnull = true;
5347					/* Report variable's declared type */
5348					*typeid = rec->rectypeid;
5349					*typetypmod = -1;
5350				}
5351				else
5352				{
5353					if (ExpandedRecordIsEmpty(rec->erh))
5354					{
5355						/* Empty record is also a NULL */
5356						*value = (Datum) 0;
5357						*isnull = true;
5358					}
5359					else
5360					{
5361						*value = ExpandedRecordGetDatum(rec->erh);
5362						*isnull = false;
5363					}
5364					if (rec->rectypeid != RECORDOID)
5365					{
5366						/* Report variable's declared type, if not RECORD */
5367						*typeid = rec->rectypeid;
5368						*typetypmod = -1;
5369					}
5370					else
5371					{
5372						/* Report record's actual type if declared RECORD */
5373						*typeid = rec->erh->er_typeid;
5374						*typetypmod = rec->erh->er_typmod;
5375					}
5376				}
5377				break;
5378			}
5379
5380		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_RECFIELD:
5381			{
5382				PLpgSQL_recfield *recfield = (PLpgSQL_recfield *) datum;
5383				PLpgSQL_rec *rec;
5384				ExpandedRecordHeader *erh;
5385
5386				rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) (estate->datums[recfield->recparentno]);
5387				erh = rec->erh;
5388
5389				/*
5390				 * If record variable is NULL, instantiate it if it has a
5391				 * named composite type, else complain.  (This won't change
5392				 * the logical state of the record: it's still NULL.)
5393				 */
5394				if (erh == NULL)
5395				{
5396					instantiate_empty_record_variable(estate, rec);
5397					erh = rec->erh;
5398				}
5399
5400				/*
5401				 * Look up the field's properties if we have not already, or
5402				 * if the tuple descriptor ID changed since last time.
5403				 */
5404				if (unlikely(recfield->rectupledescid != erh->er_tupdesc_id))
5405				{
5406					if (!expanded_record_lookup_field(erh,
5407													  recfield->fieldname,
5408													  &recfield->finfo))
5409						ereport(ERROR,
5410								(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
5411								 errmsg("record \"%s\" has no field \"%s\"",
5412										rec->refname, recfield->fieldname)));
5413					recfield->rectupledescid = erh->er_tupdesc_id;
5414				}
5415
5416				/* Report type data. */
5417				*typeid = recfield->finfo.ftypeid;
5418				*typetypmod = recfield->finfo.ftypmod;
5419
5420				/* And fetch the field value. */
5421				*value = expanded_record_get_field(erh,
5422												   recfield->finfo.fnumber,
5423												   isnull);
5424				break;
5425			}
5426
5427		default:
5428			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized dtype: %d", datum->dtype);
5429	}
5430}
5431
5432/*
5433 * plpgsql_exec_get_datum_type				Get datatype of a PLpgSQL_datum
5434 *
5435 * This is the same logic as in exec_eval_datum, but we skip acquiring
5436 * the actual value of the variable.  Also, needn't support DTYPE_ROW.
5437 */
5438Oid
5439plpgsql_exec_get_datum_type(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
5440							PLpgSQL_datum *datum)
5441{
5442	Oid			typeid;
5443
5444	switch (datum->dtype)
5445	{
5446		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR:
5447		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_PROMISE:
5448			{
5449				PLpgSQL_var *var = (PLpgSQL_var *) datum;
5450
5451				typeid = var->datatype->typoid;
5452				break;
5453			}
5454
5455		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC:
5456			{
5457				PLpgSQL_rec *rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) datum;
5458
5459				if (rec->erh == NULL || rec->rectypeid != RECORDOID)
5460				{
5461					/* Report variable's declared type */
5462					typeid = rec->rectypeid;
5463				}
5464				else
5465				{
5466					/* Report record's actual type if declared RECORD */
5467					typeid = rec->erh->er_typeid;
5468				}
5469				break;
5470			}
5471
5472		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_RECFIELD:
5473			{
5474				PLpgSQL_recfield *recfield = (PLpgSQL_recfield *) datum;
5475				PLpgSQL_rec *rec;
5476
5477				rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) (estate->datums[recfield->recparentno]);
5478
5479				/*
5480				 * If record variable is NULL, instantiate it if it has a
5481				 * named composite type, else complain.  (This won't change
5482				 * the logical state of the record: it's still NULL.)
5483				 */
5484				if (rec->erh == NULL)
5485					instantiate_empty_record_variable(estate, rec);
5486
5487				/*
5488				 * Look up the field's properties if we have not already, or
5489				 * if the tuple descriptor ID changed since last time.
5490				 */
5491				if (unlikely(recfield->rectupledescid != rec->erh->er_tupdesc_id))
5492				{
5493					if (!expanded_record_lookup_field(rec->erh,
5494													  recfield->fieldname,
5495													  &recfield->finfo))
5496						ereport(ERROR,
5497								(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
5498								 errmsg("record \"%s\" has no field \"%s\"",
5499										rec->refname, recfield->fieldname)));
5500					recfield->rectupledescid = rec->erh->er_tupdesc_id;
5501				}
5502
5503				typeid = recfield->finfo.ftypeid;
5504				break;
5505			}
5506
5507		default:
5508			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized dtype: %d", datum->dtype);
5509			typeid = InvalidOid;	/* keep compiler quiet */
5510			break;
5511	}
5512
5513	return typeid;
5514}
5515
5516/*
5517 * plpgsql_exec_get_datum_type_info			Get datatype etc of a PLpgSQL_datum
5518 *
5519 * An extended version of plpgsql_exec_get_datum_type, which also retrieves the
5520 * typmod and collation of the datum.  Note however that we don't report the
5521 * possibly-mutable typmod of RECORD values, but say -1 always.
5522 */
5523void
5524plpgsql_exec_get_datum_type_info(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
5525								 PLpgSQL_datum *datum,
5526								 Oid *typeId, int32 *typMod, Oid *collation)
5527{
5528	switch (datum->dtype)
5529	{
5530		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR:
5531		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_PROMISE:
5532			{
5533				PLpgSQL_var *var = (PLpgSQL_var *) datum;
5534
5535				*typeId = var->datatype->typoid;
5536				*typMod = var->datatype->atttypmod;
5537				*collation = var->datatype->collation;
5538				break;
5539			}
5540
5541		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC:
5542			{
5543				PLpgSQL_rec *rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) datum;
5544
5545				if (rec->erh == NULL || rec->rectypeid != RECORDOID)
5546				{
5547					/* Report variable's declared type */
5548					*typeId = rec->rectypeid;
5549					*typMod = -1;
5550				}
5551				else
5552				{
5553					/* Report record's actual type if declared RECORD */
5554					*typeId = rec->erh->er_typeid;
5555					/* do NOT return the mutable typmod of a RECORD variable */
5556					*typMod = -1;
5557				}
5558				/* composite types are never collatable */
5559				*collation = InvalidOid;
5560				break;
5561			}
5562
5563		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_RECFIELD:
5564			{
5565				PLpgSQL_recfield *recfield = (PLpgSQL_recfield *) datum;
5566				PLpgSQL_rec *rec;
5567
5568				rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) (estate->datums[recfield->recparentno]);
5569
5570				/*
5571				 * If record variable is NULL, instantiate it if it has a
5572				 * named composite type, else complain.  (This won't change
5573				 * the logical state of the record: it's still NULL.)
5574				 */
5575				if (rec->erh == NULL)
5576					instantiate_empty_record_variable(estate, rec);
5577
5578				/*
5579				 * Look up the field's properties if we have not already, or
5580				 * if the tuple descriptor ID changed since last time.
5581				 */
5582				if (unlikely(recfield->rectupledescid != rec->erh->er_tupdesc_id))
5583				{
5584					if (!expanded_record_lookup_field(rec->erh,
5585													  recfield->fieldname,
5586													  &recfield->finfo))
5587						ereport(ERROR,
5588								(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
5589								 errmsg("record \"%s\" has no field \"%s\"",
5590										rec->refname, recfield->fieldname)));
5591					recfield->rectupledescid = rec->erh->er_tupdesc_id;
5592				}
5593
5594				*typeId = recfield->finfo.ftypeid;
5595				*typMod = recfield->finfo.ftypmod;
5596				*collation = recfield->finfo.fcollation;
5597				break;
5598			}
5599
5600		default:
5601			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized dtype: %d", datum->dtype);
5602			*typeId = InvalidOid;	/* keep compiler quiet */
5603			*typMod = -1;
5604			*collation = InvalidOid;
5605			break;
5606	}
5607}
5608
5609/* ----------
5610 * exec_eval_integer		Evaluate an expression, coerce result to int4
5611 *
5612 * Note we do not do exec_eval_cleanup here; the caller must do it at
5613 * some later point.  (We do this because the caller may be holding the
5614 * results of other, pass-by-reference, expression evaluations, such as
5615 * an array value to be subscripted.)
5616 * ----------
5617 */
5618static int
5619exec_eval_integer(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
5620				  PLpgSQL_expr *expr,
5621				  bool *isNull)
5622{
5623	Datum		exprdatum;
5624	Oid			exprtypeid;
5625	int32		exprtypmod;
5626
5627	exprdatum = exec_eval_expr(estate, expr, isNull, &exprtypeid, &exprtypmod);
5628	exprdatum = exec_cast_value(estate, exprdatum, isNull,
5629								exprtypeid, exprtypmod,
5630								INT4OID, -1);
5631	return DatumGetInt32(exprdatum);
5632}
5633
5634/* ----------
5635 * exec_eval_boolean		Evaluate an expression, coerce result to bool
5636 *
5637 * Note we do not do exec_eval_cleanup here; the caller must do it at
5638 * some later point.
5639 * ----------
5640 */
5641static bool
5642exec_eval_boolean(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
5643				  PLpgSQL_expr *expr,
5644				  bool *isNull)
5645{
5646	Datum		exprdatum;
5647	Oid			exprtypeid;
5648	int32		exprtypmod;
5649
5650	exprdatum = exec_eval_expr(estate, expr, isNull, &exprtypeid, &exprtypmod);
5651	exprdatum = exec_cast_value(estate, exprdatum, isNull,
5652								exprtypeid, exprtypmod,
5653								BOOLOID, -1);
5654	return DatumGetBool(exprdatum);
5655}
5656
5657/* ----------
5658 * exec_eval_expr			Evaluate an expression and return
5659 *					the result Datum, along with data type/typmod.
5660 *
5661 * NOTE: caller must do exec_eval_cleanup when done with the Datum.
5662 * ----------
5663 */
5664static Datum
5665exec_eval_expr(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
5666			   PLpgSQL_expr *expr,
5667			   bool *isNull,
5668			   Oid *rettype,
5669			   int32 *rettypmod)
5670{
5671	Datum		result = 0;
5672	int			rc;
5673	Form_pg_attribute attr;
5674
5675	/*
5676	 * If first time through, create a plan for this expression.
5677	 */
5678	if (expr->plan == NULL)
5679		exec_prepare_plan(estate, expr, CURSOR_OPT_PARALLEL_OK);
5680
5681	/*
5682	 * If this is a simple expression, bypass SPI and use the executor
5683	 * directly
5684	 */
5685	if (exec_eval_simple_expr(estate, expr,
5686							  &result, isNull, rettype, rettypmod))
5687		return result;
5688
5689	/*
5690	 * Else do it the hard way via exec_run_select
5691	 */
5692	rc = exec_run_select(estate, expr, 0, NULL);
5693	if (rc != SPI_OK_SELECT)
5694		ereport(ERROR,
5695				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
5696				 errmsg("query did not return data"),
5697				 errcontext("query: %s", expr->query)));
5698
5699	/*
5700	 * Check that the expression returns exactly one column...
5701	 */
5702	if (estate->eval_tuptable->tupdesc->natts != 1)
5703		ereport(ERROR,
5704				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
5705				 errmsg_plural("query returned %d column",
5706							   "query returned %d columns",
5707							   estate->eval_tuptable->tupdesc->natts,
5708							   estate->eval_tuptable->tupdesc->natts),
5709				 errcontext("query: %s", expr->query)));
5710
5711	/*
5712	 * ... and get the column's datatype.
5713	 */
5714	attr = TupleDescAttr(estate->eval_tuptable->tupdesc, 0);
5715	*rettype = attr->atttypid;
5716	*rettypmod = attr->atttypmod;
5717
5718	/*
5719	 * If there are no rows selected, the result is a NULL of that type.
5720	 */
5721	if (estate->eval_processed == 0)
5722	{
5723		*isNull = true;
5724		return (Datum) 0;
5725	}
5726
5727	/*
5728	 * Check that the expression returned no more than one row.
5729	 */
5730	if (estate->eval_processed != 1)
5731		ereport(ERROR,
5732				(errcode(ERRCODE_CARDINALITY_VIOLATION),
5733				 errmsg("query returned more than one row"),
5734				 errcontext("query: %s", expr->query)));
5735
5736	/*
5737	 * Return the single result Datum.
5738	 */
5739	return SPI_getbinval(estate->eval_tuptable->vals[0],
5740						 estate->eval_tuptable->tupdesc, 1, isNull);
5741}
5742
5743
5744/* ----------
5745 * exec_run_select			Execute a select query
5746 *
5747 * Note: passing maxtuples different from 0 ("return all tuples") is
5748 * deprecated because it will prevent parallel execution of the query.
5749 * However, we retain the parameter in case we need it someday.
5750 * ----------
5751 */
5752static int
5753exec_run_select(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
5754				PLpgSQL_expr *expr, long maxtuples, Portal *portalP)
5755{
5756	ParamListInfo paramLI;
5757	int			rc;
5758
5759	/*
5760	 * On the first call for this expression generate the plan.
5761	 *
5762	 * If we don't need to return a portal, then we're just going to execute
5763	 * the query immediately, which means it's OK to use a parallel plan, even
5764	 * if the number of rows being fetched is limited.  If we do need to
5765	 * return a portal (i.e., this is for a FOR loop), the user's code might
5766	 * invoke additional operations inside the FOR loop, making parallel query
5767	 * unsafe.  In any case, we don't expect any cursor operations to be done,
5768	 * so specify NO_SCROLL for efficiency and semantic safety.
5769	 */
5770	if (expr->plan == NULL)
5771	{
5772		int			cursorOptions = CURSOR_OPT_NO_SCROLL;
5773
5774		if (portalP == NULL)
5775			cursorOptions |= CURSOR_OPT_PARALLEL_OK;
5776		exec_prepare_plan(estate, expr, cursorOptions);
5777	}
5778
5779	/*
5780	 * Set up ParamListInfo to pass to executor
5781	 */
5782	paramLI = setup_param_list(estate, expr);
5783
5784	/*
5785	 * If a portal was requested, put the query and paramlist into the portal
5786	 */
5787	if (portalP != NULL)
5788	{
5789		*portalP = SPI_cursor_open_with_paramlist(NULL, expr->plan,
5790												  paramLI,
5791												  estate->readonly_func);
5792		if (*portalP == NULL)
5793			elog(ERROR, "could not open implicit cursor for query \"%s\": %s",
5794				 expr->query, SPI_result_code_string(SPI_result));
5795		exec_eval_cleanup(estate);
5796		return SPI_OK_CURSOR;
5797	}
5798
5799	/*
5800	 * Execute the query
5801	 */
5802	rc = SPI_execute_plan_with_paramlist(expr->plan, paramLI,
5803										 estate->readonly_func, maxtuples);
5804	if (rc != SPI_OK_SELECT)
5805	{
5806		/*
5807		 * SELECT INTO deserves a special error message, because "query is not
5808		 * a SELECT" is not very helpful in that case.
5809		 */
5810		if (rc == SPI_OK_SELINTO)
5811			ereport(ERROR,
5812					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
5813					 errmsg("query is SELECT INTO, but it should be plain SELECT"),
5814					 errcontext("query: %s", expr->query)));
5815		else
5816			ereport(ERROR,
5817					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
5818					 errmsg("query is not a SELECT"),
5819					 errcontext("query: %s", expr->query)));
5820	}
5821
5822	/* Save query results for eventual cleanup */
5823	Assert(estate->eval_tuptable == NULL);
5824	estate->eval_tuptable = SPI_tuptable;
5825	estate->eval_processed = SPI_processed;
5826
5827	return rc;
5828}
5829
5830
5831/*
5832 * exec_for_query --- execute body of FOR loop for each row from a portal
5833 *
5834 * Used by exec_stmt_fors, exec_stmt_forc and exec_stmt_dynfors
5835 */
5836static int
5837exec_for_query(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, PLpgSQL_stmt_forq *stmt,
5838			   Portal portal, bool prefetch_ok)
5839{
5840	PLpgSQL_variable *var;
5841	SPITupleTable *tuptab;
5842	bool		found = false;
5843	int			rc = PLPGSQL_RC_OK;
5844	uint64		previous_id = INVALID_TUPLEDESC_IDENTIFIER;
5845	bool		tupdescs_match = true;
5846	uint64		n;
5847
5848	/* Fetch loop variable's datum entry */
5849	var = (PLpgSQL_variable *) estate->datums[stmt->var->dno];
5850
5851	/*
5852	 * Make sure the portal doesn't get closed by the user statements we
5853	 * execute.
5854	 */
5855	PinPortal(portal);
5856
5857	/*
5858	 * In a non-atomic context, we dare not prefetch, even if it would
5859	 * otherwise be safe.  Aside from any semantic hazards that that might
5860	 * create, if we prefetch toasted data and then the user commits the
5861	 * transaction, the toast references could turn into dangling pointers.
5862	 * (Rows we haven't yet fetched from the cursor are safe, because the
5863	 * PersistHoldablePortal mechanism handles this scenario.)
5864	 */
5865	if (!estate->atomic)
5866		prefetch_ok = false;
5867
5868	/*
5869	 * Fetch the initial tuple(s).  If prefetching is allowed then we grab a
5870	 * few more rows to avoid multiple trips through executor startup
5871	 * overhead.
5872	 */
5873	SPI_cursor_fetch(portal, true, prefetch_ok ? 10 : 1);
5874	tuptab = SPI_tuptable;
5875	n = SPI_processed;
5876
5877	/*
5878	 * If the query didn't return any rows, set the target to NULL and fall
5879	 * through with found = false.
5880	 */
5881	if (n == 0)
5882	{
5883		exec_move_row(estate, var, NULL, tuptab->tupdesc);
5884		exec_eval_cleanup(estate);
5885	}
5886	else
5887		found = true;			/* processed at least one tuple */
5888
5889	/*
5890	 * Now do the loop
5891	 */
5892	while (n > 0)
5893	{
5894		uint64		i;
5895
5896		for (i = 0; i < n; i++)
5897		{
5898			/*
5899			 * Assign the tuple to the target.  Here, because we know that all
5900			 * loop iterations should be assigning the same tupdesc, we can
5901			 * optimize away repeated creations of expanded records with
5902			 * identical tupdescs.  Testing for changes of er_tupdesc_id is
5903			 * reliable even if the loop body contains assignments that
5904			 * replace the target's value entirely, because it's assigned from
5905			 * a process-global counter.  The case where the tupdescs don't
5906			 * match could possibly be handled more efficiently than this
5907			 * coding does, but it's not clear extra effort is worthwhile.
5908			 */
5909			if (var->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC)
5910			{
5911				PLpgSQL_rec *rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) var;
5912
5913				if (rec->erh &&
5914					rec->erh->er_tupdesc_id == previous_id &&
5915					tupdescs_match)
5916				{
5917					/* Only need to assign a new tuple value */
5918					expanded_record_set_tuple(rec->erh, tuptab->vals[i],
5919											  true, !estate->atomic);
5920				}
5921				else
5922				{
5923					/*
5924					 * First time through, or var's tupdesc changed in loop,
5925					 * or we have to do it the hard way because type coercion
5926					 * is needed.
5927					 */
5928					exec_move_row(estate, var,
5929								  tuptab->vals[i], tuptab->tupdesc);
5930
5931					/*
5932					 * Check to see if physical assignment is OK next time.
5933					 * Once the tupdesc comparison has failed once, we don't
5934					 * bother rechecking in subsequent loop iterations.
5935					 */
5936					if (tupdescs_match)
5937					{
5938						tupdescs_match =
5939							(rec->rectypeid == RECORDOID ||
5940							 rec->rectypeid == tuptab->tupdesc->tdtypeid ||
5941							 compatible_tupdescs(tuptab->tupdesc,
5942												 expanded_record_get_tupdesc(rec->erh)));
5943					}
5944					previous_id = rec->erh->er_tupdesc_id;
5945				}
5946			}
5947			else
5948				exec_move_row(estate, var, tuptab->vals[i], tuptab->tupdesc);
5949
5950			exec_eval_cleanup(estate);
5951
5952			/*
5953			 * Execute the statements
5954			 */
5955			rc = exec_stmts(estate, stmt->body);
5956
5957			LOOP_RC_PROCESSING(stmt->label, goto loop_exit);
5958		}
5959
5960		SPI_freetuptable(tuptab);
5961
5962		/*
5963		 * Fetch more tuples.  If prefetching is allowed, grab 50 at a time.
5964		 */
5965		SPI_cursor_fetch(portal, true, prefetch_ok ? 50 : 1);
5966		tuptab = SPI_tuptable;
5967		n = SPI_processed;
5968	}
5969
5970loop_exit:
5971
5972	/*
5973	 * Release last group of tuples (if any)
5974	 */
5975	SPI_freetuptable(tuptab);
5976
5977	UnpinPortal(portal);
5978
5979	/*
5980	 * Set the FOUND variable to indicate the result of executing the loop
5981	 * (namely, whether we looped one or more times). This must be set last so
5982	 * that it does not interfere with the value of the FOUND variable inside
5983	 * the loop processing itself.
5984	 */
5985	exec_set_found(estate, found);
5986
5987	return rc;
5988}
5989
5990
5991/* ----------
5992 * exec_eval_simple_expr -		Evaluate a simple expression returning
5993 *								a Datum by directly calling ExecEvalExpr().
5994 *
5995 * If successful, store results into *result, *isNull, *rettype, *rettypmod
5996 * and return true.  If the expression cannot be handled by simple evaluation,
5997 * return false.
5998 *
5999 * Because we only store one execution tree for a simple expression, we
6000 * can't handle recursion cases.  So, if we see the tree is already busy
6001 * with an evaluation in the current xact, we just return false and let the
6002 * caller run the expression the hard way.  (Other alternatives such as
6003 * creating a new tree for a recursive call either introduce memory leaks,
6004 * or add enough bookkeeping to be doubtful wins anyway.)  Another case that
6005 * is covered by the expr_simple_in_use test is where a previous execution
6006 * of the tree was aborted by an error: the tree may contain bogus state
6007 * so we dare not re-use it.
6008 *
6009 * It is possible that we'd need to replan a simple expression; for example,
6010 * someone might redefine a SQL function that had been inlined into the simple
6011 * expression.  That cannot cause a simple expression to become non-simple (or
6012 * vice versa), but we do have to handle replacing the expression tree.
6013 *
6014 * Note: if pass-by-reference, the result is in the eval_mcontext.
6015 * It will be freed when exec_eval_cleanup is done.
6016 * ----------
6017 */
6018static bool
6019exec_eval_simple_expr(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
6020					  PLpgSQL_expr *expr,
6021					  Datum *result,
6022					  bool *isNull,
6023					  Oid *rettype,
6024					  int32 *rettypmod)
6025{
6026	ExprContext *econtext = estate->eval_econtext;
6027	LocalTransactionId curlxid = MyProc->vxid.lxid;
6028	ParamListInfo paramLI;
6029	void	   *save_setup_arg;
6030	bool		need_snapshot;
6031	MemoryContext oldcontext;
6032
6033	/*
6034	 * Forget it if expression wasn't simple before.
6035	 */
6036	if (expr->expr_simple_expr == NULL)
6037		return false;
6038
6039	/*
6040	 * If expression is in use in current xact, don't touch it.
6041	 */
6042	if (unlikely(expr->expr_simple_in_use) &&
6043		expr->expr_simple_lxid == curlxid)
6044		return false;
6045
6046	/*
6047	 * Ensure that there's a portal-level snapshot, in case this simple
6048	 * expression is the first thing evaluated after a COMMIT or ROLLBACK.
6049	 * We'd have to do this anyway before executing the expression, so we
6050	 * might as well do it now to ensure that any possible replanning doesn't
6051	 * need to take a new snapshot.
6052	 */
6053	EnsurePortalSnapshotExists();
6054
6055	/*
6056	 * Check to see if the cached plan has been invalidated.  If not, and this
6057	 * is the first use in the current transaction, save a plan refcount in
6058	 * the simple-expression resowner.
6059	 */
6060	if (likely(CachedPlanIsSimplyValid(expr->expr_simple_plansource,
6061									   expr->expr_simple_plan,
6062									   (expr->expr_simple_plan_lxid != curlxid ?
6063										estate->simple_eval_resowner : NULL))))
6064	{
6065		/*
6066		 * It's still good, so just remember that we have a refcount on the
6067		 * plan in the current transaction.  (If we already had one, this
6068		 * assignment is a no-op.)
6069		 */
6070		expr->expr_simple_plan_lxid = curlxid;
6071	}
6072	else
6073	{
6074		/* Need to replan */
6075		CachedPlan *cplan;
6076
6077		/*
6078		 * If we have a valid refcount on some previous version of the plan,
6079		 * release it, so we don't leak plans intra-transaction.
6080		 */
6081		if (expr->expr_simple_plan_lxid == curlxid)
6082			ReleaseCachedPlan(expr->expr_simple_plan,
6083							  estate->simple_eval_resowner);
6084
6085		/*
6086		 * Reset to "not simple" to leave sane state (with no dangling
6087		 * pointers) in case we fail while replanning.  We'll need to
6088		 * re-determine simplicity and R/W optimizability anyway, since those
6089		 * could change with the new plan.  expr_simple_plansource can be left
6090		 * alone however, as that cannot move.
6091		 */
6092		expr->expr_simple_expr = NULL;
6093		expr->expr_rwopt = PLPGSQL_RWOPT_UNKNOWN;
6094		expr->expr_rw_param = NULL;
6095		expr->expr_simple_plan = NULL;
6096		expr->expr_simple_plan_lxid = InvalidLocalTransactionId;
6097
6098		/* Do the replanning work in the eval_mcontext */
6099		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
6100		cplan = SPI_plan_get_cached_plan(expr->plan);
6101		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
6102
6103		/*
6104		 * We can't get a failure here, because the number of
6105		 * CachedPlanSources in the SPI plan can't change from what
6106		 * exec_simple_check_plan saw; it's a property of the raw parsetree
6107		 * generated from the query text.
6108		 */
6109		Assert(cplan != NULL);
6110
6111		/*
6112		 * Recheck exec_is_simple_query, which could now report false in
6113		 * edge-case scenarios such as a non-SRF having been replaced with a
6114		 * SRF.  Also recheck CachedPlanAllowsSimpleValidityCheck, just to be
6115		 * sure.  If either test fails, cope by declaring the plan to be
6116		 * non-simple.  On success, we'll acquire a refcount on the new plan,
6117		 * stored in simple_eval_resowner.
6118		 */
6119		if (exec_is_simple_query(expr) &&
6120			CachedPlanAllowsSimpleValidityCheck(expr->expr_simple_plansource,
6121												cplan,
6122												estate->simple_eval_resowner))
6123		{
6124			/* Remember that we have the refcount */
6125			expr->expr_simple_plan = cplan;
6126			expr->expr_simple_plan_lxid = curlxid;
6127		}
6128		else
6129		{
6130			/* Release SPI_plan_get_cached_plan's refcount */
6131			ReleaseCachedPlan(cplan, CurrentResourceOwner);
6132			return false;
6133		}
6134
6135		/*
6136		 * SPI_plan_get_cached_plan acquired a plan refcount stored in the
6137		 * active resowner.  We don't need that anymore, so release it.
6138		 */
6139		ReleaseCachedPlan(cplan, CurrentResourceOwner);
6140
6141		/* Extract desired scalar expression from cached plan */
6142		exec_save_simple_expr(expr, cplan);
6143	}
6144
6145	/*
6146	 * Pass back previously-determined result type.
6147	 */
6148	*rettype = expr->expr_simple_type;
6149	*rettypmod = expr->expr_simple_typmod;
6150
6151	/*
6152	 * Set up ParamListInfo to pass to executor.  For safety, save and restore
6153	 * estate->paramLI->parserSetupArg around our use of the param list.
6154	 */
6155	paramLI = estate->paramLI;
6156	save_setup_arg = paramLI->parserSetupArg;
6157
6158	/*
6159	 * We can skip using setup_param_list() in favor of just doing this
6160	 * unconditionally, because there's no need for the optimization of
6161	 * possibly setting ecxt_param_list_info to NULL; we've already forced use
6162	 * of a generic plan.
6163	 */
6164	paramLI->parserSetupArg = expr;
6165	econtext->ecxt_param_list_info = paramLI;
6166
6167	/*
6168	 * Prepare the expression for execution, if it's not been done already in
6169	 * the current transaction.  (This will be forced to happen if we called
6170	 * exec_save_simple_expr above.)
6171	 */
6172	if (unlikely(expr->expr_simple_lxid != curlxid))
6173	{
6174		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(estate->simple_eval_estate->es_query_cxt);
6175		expr->expr_simple_state =
6176			ExecInitExprWithParams(expr->expr_simple_expr,
6177								   econtext->ecxt_param_list_info);
6178		expr->expr_simple_in_use = false;
6179		expr->expr_simple_lxid = curlxid;
6180		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
6181	}
6182
6183	/*
6184	 * We have to do some of the things SPI_execute_plan would do, in
6185	 * particular push a new snapshot so that stable functions within the
6186	 * expression can see updates made so far by our own function.  However,
6187	 * we can skip doing that (and just invoke the expression with the same
6188	 * snapshot passed to our function) in some cases, which is useful because
6189	 * it's quite expensive relative to the cost of a simple expression.  We
6190	 * can skip it if the expression contains no stable or volatile functions;
6191	 * immutable functions shouldn't need to see our updates.  Also, if this
6192	 * is a read-only function, we haven't made any updates so again it's okay
6193	 * to skip.
6194	 */
6195	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
6196	need_snapshot = (expr->expr_simple_mutable && !estate->readonly_func);
6197	if (need_snapshot)
6198	{
6199		CommandCounterIncrement();
6200		PushActiveSnapshot(GetTransactionSnapshot());
6201	}
6202
6203	/*
6204	 * Mark expression as busy for the duration of the ExecEvalExpr call.
6205	 */
6206	expr->expr_simple_in_use = true;
6207
6208	/*
6209	 * Finally we can call the executor to evaluate the expression
6210	 */
6211	*result = ExecEvalExpr(expr->expr_simple_state,
6212						   econtext,
6213						   isNull);
6214
6215	/* Assorted cleanup */
6216	expr->expr_simple_in_use = false;
6217
6218	econtext->ecxt_param_list_info = NULL;
6219
6220	paramLI->parserSetupArg = save_setup_arg;
6221
6222	if (need_snapshot)
6223		PopActiveSnapshot();
6224
6225	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
6226
6227	/*
6228	 * That's it.
6229	 */
6230	return true;
6231}
6232
6233
6234/*
6235 * Create a ParamListInfo to pass to SPI
6236 *
6237 * We use a single ParamListInfo struct for all SPI calls made to evaluate
6238 * PLpgSQL_exprs in this estate.  It contains no per-param data, just hook
6239 * functions, so it's effectively read-only for SPI.
6240 *
6241 * An exception from pure read-only-ness is that the parserSetupArg points
6242 * to the specific PLpgSQL_expr being evaluated.  This is not an issue for
6243 * statement-level callers, but lower-level callers must save and restore
6244 * estate->paramLI->parserSetupArg just in case there's an active evaluation
6245 * at an outer call level.  (A plausible alternative design would be to
6246 * create a ParamListInfo struct for each PLpgSQL_expr, but for the moment
6247 * that seems like a waste of memory.)
6248 */
6249static ParamListInfo
6250setup_param_list(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, PLpgSQL_expr *expr)
6251{
6252	ParamListInfo paramLI;
6253
6254	/*
6255	 * We must have created the SPIPlan already (hence, query text has been
6256	 * parsed/analyzed at least once); else we cannot rely on expr->paramnos.
6257	 */
6258	Assert(expr->plan != NULL);
6259
6260	/*
6261	 * We only need a ParamListInfo if the expression has parameters.
6262	 */
6263	if (!bms_is_empty(expr->paramnos))
6264	{
6265		/* Use the common ParamListInfo */
6266		paramLI = estate->paramLI;
6267
6268		/*
6269		 * Set up link to active expr where the hook functions can find it.
6270		 * Callers must save and restore parserSetupArg if there is any chance
6271		 * that they are interrupting an active use of parameters.
6272		 */
6273		paramLI->parserSetupArg = expr;
6274	}
6275	else
6276	{
6277		/*
6278		 * Expression requires no parameters.  Be sure we represent this case
6279		 * as a NULL ParamListInfo, so that plancache.c knows there is no
6280		 * point in a custom plan.
6281		 */
6282		paramLI = NULL;
6283	}
6284	return paramLI;
6285}
6286
6287/*
6288 * plpgsql_param_fetch		paramFetch callback for dynamic parameter fetch
6289 *
6290 * We always use the caller's workspace to construct the returned struct.
6291 *
6292 * Note: this is no longer used during query execution.  It is used during
6293 * planning (with speculative == true) and when the ParamListInfo we supply
6294 * to the executor is copied into a cursor portal or transferred to a
6295 * parallel child process.
6296 */
6297static ParamExternData *
6298plpgsql_param_fetch(ParamListInfo params,
6299					int paramid, bool speculative,
6300					ParamExternData *prm)
6301{
6302	int			dno;
6303	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6304	PLpgSQL_expr *expr;
6305	PLpgSQL_datum *datum;
6306	bool		ok = true;
6307	int32		prmtypmod;
6308
6309	/* paramid's are 1-based, but dnos are 0-based */
6310	dno = paramid - 1;
6311	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < params->numParams);
6312
6313	/* fetch back the hook data */
6314	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6315	expr = (PLpgSQL_expr *) params->parserSetupArg;
6316	Assert(params->numParams == estate->ndatums);
6317
6318	/* now we can access the target datum */
6319	datum = estate->datums[dno];
6320
6321	/*
6322	 * Since copyParamList() or SerializeParamList() will try to materialize
6323	 * every single parameter slot, it's important to return a dummy param
6324	 * when asked for a datum that's not supposed to be used by this SQL
6325	 * expression.  Otherwise we risk failures in exec_eval_datum(), or
6326	 * copying a lot more data than necessary.
6327	 */
6328	if (!bms_is_member(dno, expr->paramnos))
6329		ok = false;
6330
6331	/*
6332	 * If the access is speculative, we prefer to return no data rather than
6333	 * to fail in exec_eval_datum().  Check the likely failure cases.
6334	 */
6335	else if (speculative)
6336	{
6337		switch (datum->dtype)
6338		{
6339			case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR:
6340			case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_PROMISE:
6341				/* always safe */
6342				break;
6343
6344			case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_ROW:
6345				/* should be safe in all interesting cases */
6346				break;
6347
6348			case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC:
6349				/* always safe (might return NULL, that's fine) */
6350				break;
6351
6352			case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_RECFIELD:
6353				{
6354					PLpgSQL_recfield *recfield = (PLpgSQL_recfield *) datum;
6355					PLpgSQL_rec *rec;
6356
6357					rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) (estate->datums[recfield->recparentno]);
6358
6359					/*
6360					 * If record variable is NULL, don't risk anything.
6361					 */
6362					if (rec->erh == NULL)
6363						ok = false;
6364
6365					/*
6366					 * Look up the field's properties if we have not already,
6367					 * or if the tuple descriptor ID changed since last time.
6368					 */
6369					else if (unlikely(recfield->rectupledescid != rec->erh->er_tupdesc_id))
6370					{
6371						if (expanded_record_lookup_field(rec->erh,
6372														 recfield->fieldname,
6373														 &recfield->finfo))
6374							recfield->rectupledescid = rec->erh->er_tupdesc_id;
6375						else
6376							ok = false;
6377					}
6378					break;
6379				}
6380
6381			default:
6382				ok = false;
6383				break;
6384		}
6385	}
6386
6387	/* Return "no such parameter" if not ok */
6388	if (!ok)
6389	{
6390		prm->value = (Datum) 0;
6391		prm->isnull = true;
6392		prm->pflags = 0;
6393		prm->ptype = InvalidOid;
6394		return prm;
6395	}
6396
6397	/* OK, evaluate the value and store into the return struct */
6398	exec_eval_datum(estate, datum,
6399					&prm->ptype, &prmtypmod,
6400					&prm->value, &prm->isnull);
6401	/* We can always mark params as "const" for executor's purposes */
6402	prm->pflags = PARAM_FLAG_CONST;
6403
6404	/*
6405	 * If it's a read/write expanded datum, convert reference to read-only.
6406	 * (There's little point in trying to optimize read/write parameters,
6407	 * given the cases in which this function is used.)
6408	 */
6409	if (datum->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR)
6410		prm->value = MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly(prm->value,
6411												prm->isnull,
6412												((PLpgSQL_var *) datum)->datatype->typlen);
6413	else if (datum->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC)
6414		prm->value = MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly(prm->value,
6415												prm->isnull,
6416												-1);
6417
6418	return prm;
6419}
6420
6421/*
6422 * plpgsql_param_compile		paramCompile callback for plpgsql parameters
6423 */
6424static void
6425plpgsql_param_compile(ParamListInfo params, Param *param,
6426					  ExprState *state,
6427					  Datum *resv, bool *resnull)
6428{
6429	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6430	PLpgSQL_expr *expr;
6431	int			dno;
6432	PLpgSQL_datum *datum;
6433	ExprEvalStep scratch;
6434
6435	/* fetch back the hook data */
6436	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6437	expr = (PLpgSQL_expr *) params->parserSetupArg;
6438
6439	/* paramid's are 1-based, but dnos are 0-based */
6440	dno = param->paramid - 1;
6441	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
6442
6443	/* now we can access the target datum */
6444	datum = estate->datums[dno];
6445
6446	scratch.opcode = EEOP_PARAM_CALLBACK;
6447	scratch.resvalue = resv;
6448	scratch.resnull = resnull;
6449
6450	/*
6451	 * Select appropriate eval function.
6452	 *
6453	 * First, if this Param references the same varlena-type DTYPE_VAR datum
6454	 * that is the target of the assignment containing this simple expression,
6455	 * then it's possible we will be able to optimize handling of R/W expanded
6456	 * datums.  We don't want to do the work needed to determine that unless
6457	 * we actually see a R/W expanded datum at runtime, so install a checking
6458	 * function that will figure that out when needed.
6459	 *
6460	 * Otherwise, it seems worth special-casing DTYPE_VAR and DTYPE_RECFIELD
6461	 * for performance.  Also, we can determine in advance whether
6462	 * MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly() will be required.  Currently, only
6463	 * VAR/PROMISE and REC datums could contain read/write expanded objects.
6464	 */
6465	if (datum->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR)
6466	{
6467		bool		isvarlena = (((PLpgSQL_var *) datum)->datatype->typlen == -1);
6468
6469		if (isvarlena && dno == expr->target_param && expr->expr_simple_expr)
6470			scratch.d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_var_check;
6471		else if (isvarlena)
6472			scratch.d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_var_ro;
6473		else
6474			scratch.d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_var;
6475	}
6476	else if (datum->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_RECFIELD)
6477		scratch.d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_recfield;
6478	else if (datum->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_PROMISE)
6479	{
6480		if (((PLpgSQL_var *) datum)->datatype->typlen == -1)
6481			scratch.d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_generic_ro;
6482		else
6483			scratch.d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_generic;
6484	}
6485	else if (datum->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC)
6486		scratch.d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_generic_ro;
6487	else
6488		scratch.d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_generic;
6489
6490	/*
6491	 * Note: it's tempting to use paramarg to store the estate pointer and
6492	 * thereby save an indirection or two in the eval functions.  But that
6493	 * doesn't work because the compiled expression might be used with
6494	 * different estates for the same PL/pgSQL function.  Instead, store
6495	 * pointers to the PLpgSQL_expr as well as this specific Param, to support
6496	 * plpgsql_param_eval_var_check().
6497	 */
6498	scratch.d.cparam.paramarg = expr;
6499	scratch.d.cparam.paramarg2 = param;
6500	scratch.d.cparam.paramid = param->paramid;
6501	scratch.d.cparam.paramtype = param->paramtype;
6502	ExprEvalPushStep(state, &scratch);
6503}
6504
6505/*
6506 * plpgsql_param_eval_var_check		evaluation of EEOP_PARAM_CALLBACK step
6507 *
6508 * This is specialized to the case of DTYPE_VAR variables for which
6509 * we may need to determine the applicability of a read/write optimization,
6510 * but we've not done that yet.  The work to determine applicability will
6511 * be done at most once (per construction of the PL/pgSQL function's cache
6512 * entry) when we first see that the target variable's old value is a R/W
6513 * expanded object.  If we never do see that, nothing is lost: the amount
6514 * of work done by this function in that case is just about the same as
6515 * what would be done by plpgsql_param_eval_var_ro, which is what we'd
6516 * have used otherwise.
6517 */
6518static void
6519plpgsql_param_eval_var_check(ExprState *state, ExprEvalStep *op,
6520							 ExprContext *econtext)
6521{
6522	ParamListInfo params;
6523	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6524	int			dno = op->d.cparam.paramid - 1;
6525	PLpgSQL_var *var;
6526
6527	/* fetch back the hook data */
6528	params = econtext->ecxt_param_list_info;
6529	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6530	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
6531
6532	/* now we can access the target datum */
6533	var = (PLpgSQL_var *) estate->datums[dno];
6534	Assert(var->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR);
6535
6536	/*
6537	 * If the variable's current value is a R/W expanded object, it's time to
6538	 * decide whether/how to optimize the assignment.
6539	 */
6540	if (!var->isnull &&
6541		VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL_EXPANDED_RW(DatumGetPointer(var->value)))
6542	{
6543		PLpgSQL_expr *expr = (PLpgSQL_expr *) op->d.cparam.paramarg;
6544		Param	   *param = (Param *) op->d.cparam.paramarg2;
6545
6546		/*
6547		 * We might have already figured this out while evaluating some other
6548		 * Param referencing the same variable, so check expr_rwopt first.
6549		 */
6550		if (expr->expr_rwopt == PLPGSQL_RWOPT_UNKNOWN)
6551			exec_check_rw_parameter(expr, op->d.cparam.paramid);
6552
6553		/*
6554		 * Update the callback pointer to match what we decided to do, so that
6555		 * this function will not be called again.  Then pass off this
6556		 * execution to the newly-selected function.
6557		 */
6558		switch (expr->expr_rwopt)
6559		{
6560			case PLPGSQL_RWOPT_UNKNOWN:
6561				Assert(false);
6562				break;
6563			case PLPGSQL_RWOPT_NOPE:
6564				/* Force the value to read-only in all future executions */
6565				op->d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_var_ro;
6566				plpgsql_param_eval_var_ro(state, op, econtext);
6567				break;
6568			case PLPGSQL_RWOPT_TRANSFER:
6569				/* There can be only one matching Param in this case */
6570				Assert(param == expr->expr_rw_param);
6571				/* When the value is read/write, transfer to exec context */
6572				op->d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_var_transfer;
6573				plpgsql_param_eval_var_transfer(state, op, econtext);
6574				break;
6575			case PLPGSQL_RWOPT_INPLACE:
6576				if (param == expr->expr_rw_param)
6577				{
6578					/* When the value is read/write, deliver it as-is */
6579					op->d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_var;
6580					plpgsql_param_eval_var(state, op, econtext);
6581				}
6582				else
6583				{
6584					/* Not the optimizable reference, so force to read-only */
6585					op->d.cparam.paramfunc = plpgsql_param_eval_var_ro;
6586					plpgsql_param_eval_var_ro(state, op, econtext);
6587				}
6588				break;
6589		}
6590		return;
6591	}
6592
6593	/*
6594	 * Otherwise, continue to postpone that decision, and execute an inlined
6595	 * version of exec_eval_datum().  Although this value could potentially
6596	 * need MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly, we know it doesn't right now.
6597	 */
6598	*op->resvalue = var->value;
6599	*op->resnull = var->isnull;
6600
6601	/* safety check -- an assertion should be sufficient */
6602	Assert(var->datatype->typoid == op->d.cparam.paramtype);
6603}
6604
6605/*
6606 * plpgsql_param_eval_var_transfer		evaluation of EEOP_PARAM_CALLBACK step
6607 *
6608 * This is specialized to the case of DTYPE_VAR variables for which
6609 * we have determined that a read/write expanded value can be handed off
6610 * into execution of the expression (and then possibly returned to our
6611 * function's ownership afterwards).  We have to test though, because the
6612 * variable might not contain a read/write expanded value during this
6613 * execution.
6614 */
6615static void
6616plpgsql_param_eval_var_transfer(ExprState *state, ExprEvalStep *op,
6617								ExprContext *econtext)
6618{
6619	ParamListInfo params;
6620	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6621	int			dno = op->d.cparam.paramid - 1;
6622	PLpgSQL_var *var;
6623
6624	/* fetch back the hook data */
6625	params = econtext->ecxt_param_list_info;
6626	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6627	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
6628
6629	/* now we can access the target datum */
6630	var = (PLpgSQL_var *) estate->datums[dno];
6631	Assert(var->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR);
6632
6633	/*
6634	 * If the variable's current value is a R/W expanded object, transfer its
6635	 * ownership into the expression execution context, then drop our own
6636	 * reference to the value by setting the variable to NULL.  That'll be
6637	 * overwritten (perhaps with this same object) when control comes back
6638	 * from the expression.
6639	 */
6640	if (!var->isnull &&
6641		VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL_EXPANDED_RW(DatumGetPointer(var->value)))
6642	{
6643		*op->resvalue = TransferExpandedObject(var->value,
6644											   get_eval_mcontext(estate));
6645		*op->resnull = false;
6646
6647		var->value = (Datum) 0;
6648		var->isnull = true;
6649		var->freeval = false;
6650	}
6651	else
6652	{
6653		/*
6654		 * Otherwise we can pass the variable's value directly; we now know
6655		 * that MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly isn't needed.
6656		 */
6657		*op->resvalue = var->value;
6658		*op->resnull = var->isnull;
6659	}
6660
6661	/* safety check -- an assertion should be sufficient */
6662	Assert(var->datatype->typoid == op->d.cparam.paramtype);
6663}
6664
6665/*
6666 * plpgsql_param_eval_var		evaluation of EEOP_PARAM_CALLBACK step
6667 *
6668 * This is specialized to the case of DTYPE_VAR variables for which
6669 * we do not need to invoke MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly.
6670 */
6671static void
6672plpgsql_param_eval_var(ExprState *state, ExprEvalStep *op,
6673					   ExprContext *econtext)
6674{
6675	ParamListInfo params;
6676	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6677	int			dno = op->d.cparam.paramid - 1;
6678	PLpgSQL_var *var;
6679
6680	/* fetch back the hook data */
6681	params = econtext->ecxt_param_list_info;
6682	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6683	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
6684
6685	/* now we can access the target datum */
6686	var = (PLpgSQL_var *) estate->datums[dno];
6687	Assert(var->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR);
6688
6689	/* inlined version of exec_eval_datum() */
6690	*op->resvalue = var->value;
6691	*op->resnull = var->isnull;
6692
6693	/* safety check -- an assertion should be sufficient */
6694	Assert(var->datatype->typoid == op->d.cparam.paramtype);
6695}
6696
6697/*
6698 * plpgsql_param_eval_var_ro		evaluation of EEOP_PARAM_CALLBACK step
6699 *
6700 * This is specialized to the case of DTYPE_VAR variables for which
6701 * we need to invoke MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly.
6702 */
6703static void
6704plpgsql_param_eval_var_ro(ExprState *state, ExprEvalStep *op,
6705						  ExprContext *econtext)
6706{
6707	ParamListInfo params;
6708	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6709	int			dno = op->d.cparam.paramid - 1;
6710	PLpgSQL_var *var;
6711
6712	/* fetch back the hook data */
6713	params = econtext->ecxt_param_list_info;
6714	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6715	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
6716
6717	/* now we can access the target datum */
6718	var = (PLpgSQL_var *) estate->datums[dno];
6719	Assert(var->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR);
6720
6721	/*
6722	 * Inlined version of exec_eval_datum() ... and while we're at it, force
6723	 * expanded datums to read-only.
6724	 */
6725	*op->resvalue = MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly(var->value,
6726											   var->isnull,
6727											   -1);
6728	*op->resnull = var->isnull;
6729
6730	/* safety check -- an assertion should be sufficient */
6731	Assert(var->datatype->typoid == op->d.cparam.paramtype);
6732}
6733
6734/*
6735 * plpgsql_param_eval_recfield		evaluation of EEOP_PARAM_CALLBACK step
6736 *
6737 * This is specialized to the case of DTYPE_RECFIELD variables, for which
6738 * we never need to invoke MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly.
6739 */
6740static void
6741plpgsql_param_eval_recfield(ExprState *state, ExprEvalStep *op,
6742							ExprContext *econtext)
6743{
6744	ParamListInfo params;
6745	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6746	int			dno = op->d.cparam.paramid - 1;
6747	PLpgSQL_recfield *recfield;
6748	PLpgSQL_rec *rec;
6749	ExpandedRecordHeader *erh;
6750
6751	/* fetch back the hook data */
6752	params = econtext->ecxt_param_list_info;
6753	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6754	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
6755
6756	/* now we can access the target datum */
6757	recfield = (PLpgSQL_recfield *) estate->datums[dno];
6758	Assert(recfield->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_RECFIELD);
6759
6760	/* inline the relevant part of exec_eval_datum */
6761	rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) (estate->datums[recfield->recparentno]);
6762	erh = rec->erh;
6763
6764	/*
6765	 * If record variable is NULL, instantiate it if it has a named composite
6766	 * type, else complain.  (This won't change the logical state of the
6767	 * record: it's still NULL.)
6768	 */
6769	if (erh == NULL)
6770	{
6771		instantiate_empty_record_variable(estate, rec);
6772		erh = rec->erh;
6773	}
6774
6775	/*
6776	 * Look up the field's properties if we have not already, or if the tuple
6777	 * descriptor ID changed since last time.
6778	 */
6779	if (unlikely(recfield->rectupledescid != erh->er_tupdesc_id))
6780	{
6781		if (!expanded_record_lookup_field(erh,
6782										  recfield->fieldname,
6783										  &recfield->finfo))
6784			ereport(ERROR,
6785					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
6786					 errmsg("record \"%s\" has no field \"%s\"",
6787							rec->refname, recfield->fieldname)));
6788		recfield->rectupledescid = erh->er_tupdesc_id;
6789	}
6790
6791	/* OK to fetch the field value. */
6792	*op->resvalue = expanded_record_get_field(erh,
6793											  recfield->finfo.fnumber,
6794											  op->resnull);
6795
6796	/* safety check -- needed for, eg, record fields */
6797	if (unlikely(recfield->finfo.ftypeid != op->d.cparam.paramtype))
6798		ereport(ERROR,
6799				(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
6800				 errmsg("type of parameter %d (%s) does not match that when preparing the plan (%s)",
6801						op->d.cparam.paramid,
6802						format_type_be(recfield->finfo.ftypeid),
6803						format_type_be(op->d.cparam.paramtype))));
6804}
6805
6806/*
6807 * plpgsql_param_eval_generic		evaluation of EEOP_PARAM_CALLBACK step
6808 *
6809 * This handles all variable types, but assumes we do not need to invoke
6810 * MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly.
6811 */
6812static void
6813plpgsql_param_eval_generic(ExprState *state, ExprEvalStep *op,
6814						   ExprContext *econtext)
6815{
6816	ParamListInfo params;
6817	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6818	int			dno = op->d.cparam.paramid - 1;
6819	PLpgSQL_datum *datum;
6820	Oid			datumtype;
6821	int32		datumtypmod;
6822
6823	/* fetch back the hook data */
6824	params = econtext->ecxt_param_list_info;
6825	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6826	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
6827
6828	/* now we can access the target datum */
6829	datum = estate->datums[dno];
6830
6831	/* fetch datum's value */
6832	exec_eval_datum(estate, datum,
6833					&datumtype, &datumtypmod,
6834					op->resvalue, op->resnull);
6835
6836	/* safety check -- needed for, eg, record fields */
6837	if (unlikely(datumtype != op->d.cparam.paramtype))
6838		ereport(ERROR,
6839				(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
6840				 errmsg("type of parameter %d (%s) does not match that when preparing the plan (%s)",
6841						op->d.cparam.paramid,
6842						format_type_be(datumtype),
6843						format_type_be(op->d.cparam.paramtype))));
6844}
6845
6846/*
6847 * plpgsql_param_eval_generic_ro	evaluation of EEOP_PARAM_CALLBACK step
6848 *
6849 * This handles all variable types, but assumes we need to invoke
6850 * MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly (hence, variable must be of a varlena type).
6851 */
6852static void
6853plpgsql_param_eval_generic_ro(ExprState *state, ExprEvalStep *op,
6854							  ExprContext *econtext)
6855{
6856	ParamListInfo params;
6857	PLpgSQL_execstate *estate;
6858	int			dno = op->d.cparam.paramid - 1;
6859	PLpgSQL_datum *datum;
6860	Oid			datumtype;
6861	int32		datumtypmod;
6862
6863	/* fetch back the hook data */
6864	params = econtext->ecxt_param_list_info;
6865	estate = (PLpgSQL_execstate *) params->paramFetchArg;
6866	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
6867
6868	/* now we can access the target datum */
6869	datum = estate->datums[dno];
6870
6871	/* fetch datum's value */
6872	exec_eval_datum(estate, datum,
6873					&datumtype, &datumtypmod,
6874					op->resvalue, op->resnull);
6875
6876	/* safety check -- needed for, eg, record fields */
6877	if (unlikely(datumtype != op->d.cparam.paramtype))
6878		ereport(ERROR,
6879				(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
6880				 errmsg("type of parameter %d (%s) does not match that when preparing the plan (%s)",
6881						op->d.cparam.paramid,
6882						format_type_be(datumtype),
6883						format_type_be(op->d.cparam.paramtype))));
6884
6885	/* force the value to read-only */
6886	*op->resvalue = MakeExpandedObjectReadOnly(*op->resvalue,
6887											   *op->resnull,
6888											   -1);
6889}
6890
6891
6892/*
6893 * exec_move_row			Move one tuple's values into a record or row
6894 *
6895 * tup and tupdesc may both be NULL if we're just assigning an indeterminate
6896 * composite NULL to the target.  Alternatively, can have tup be NULL and
6897 * tupdesc not NULL, in which case we assign a row of NULLs to the target.
6898 *
6899 * Since this uses the mcontext for workspace, caller should eventually call
6900 * exec_eval_cleanup to prevent long-term memory leaks.
6901 */
6902static void
6903exec_move_row(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
6904			  PLpgSQL_variable *target,
6905			  HeapTuple tup, TupleDesc tupdesc)
6906{
6907	ExpandedRecordHeader *newerh = NULL;
6908
6909	/*
6910	 * If target is RECORD, we may be able to avoid field-by-field processing.
6911	 */
6912	if (target->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC)
6913	{
6914		PLpgSQL_rec *rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) target;
6915
6916		/*
6917		 * If we have no source tupdesc, just set the record variable to NULL.
6918		 * (If we have a source tupdesc but not a tuple, we'll set the
6919		 * variable to a row of nulls, instead.  This is odd perhaps, but
6920		 * backwards compatible.)
6921		 */
6922		if (tupdesc == NULL)
6923		{
6924			if (rec->datatype &&
6925				rec->datatype->typtype == TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
6926			{
6927				/*
6928				 * If it's a composite domain, NULL might not be a legal
6929				 * value, so we instead need to make an empty expanded record
6930				 * and ensure that domain type checking gets done.  If there
6931				 * is already an expanded record, piggyback on its lookups.
6932				 */
6933				newerh = make_expanded_record_for_rec(estate, rec,
6934													  NULL, rec->erh);
6935				expanded_record_set_tuple(newerh, NULL, false, false);
6936				assign_record_var(estate, rec, newerh);
6937			}
6938			else
6939			{
6940				/* Just clear it to NULL */
6941				if (rec->erh)
6942					DeleteExpandedObject(ExpandedRecordGetDatum(rec->erh));
6943				rec->erh = NULL;
6944			}
6945			return;
6946		}
6947
6948		/*
6949		 * Build a new expanded record with appropriate tupdesc.
6950		 */
6951		newerh = make_expanded_record_for_rec(estate, rec, tupdesc, NULL);
6952
6953		/*
6954		 * If the rowtypes match, or if we have no tuple anyway, we can
6955		 * complete the assignment without field-by-field processing.
6956		 *
6957		 * The tests here are ordered more or less in order of cheapness.  We
6958		 * can easily detect it will work if the target is declared RECORD or
6959		 * has the same typeid as the source.  But when assigning from a query
6960		 * result, it's common to have a source tupdesc that's labeled RECORD
6961		 * but is actually physically compatible with a named-composite-type
6962		 * target, so it's worth spending extra cycles to check for that.
6963		 */
6964		if (rec->rectypeid == RECORDOID ||
6965			rec->rectypeid == tupdesc->tdtypeid ||
6966			!HeapTupleIsValid(tup) ||
6967			compatible_tupdescs(tupdesc, expanded_record_get_tupdesc(newerh)))
6968		{
6969			if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
6970			{
6971				/* No data, so force the record into all-nulls state */
6972				deconstruct_expanded_record(newerh);
6973			}
6974			else
6975			{
6976				/* No coercion is needed, so just assign the row value */
6977				expanded_record_set_tuple(newerh, tup, true, !estate->atomic);
6978			}
6979
6980			/* Complete the assignment */
6981			assign_record_var(estate, rec, newerh);
6982
6983			return;
6984		}
6985	}
6986
6987	/*
6988	 * Otherwise, deconstruct the tuple and do field-by-field assignment,
6989	 * using exec_move_row_from_fields.
6990	 */
6991	if (tupdesc && HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
6992	{
6993		int			td_natts = tupdesc->natts;
6994		Datum	   *values;
6995		bool	   *nulls;
6996		Datum		values_local[64];
6997		bool		nulls_local[64];
6998
6999		/*
7000		 * Need workspace arrays.  If td_natts is small enough, use local
7001		 * arrays to save doing a palloc.  Even if it's not small, we can
7002		 * allocate both the Datum and isnull arrays in one palloc chunk.
7003		 */
7004		if (td_natts <= lengthof(values_local))
7005		{
7006			values = values_local;
7007			nulls = nulls_local;
7008		}
7009		else
7010		{
7011			char	   *chunk;
7012
7013			chunk = eval_mcontext_alloc(estate,
7014										td_natts * (sizeof(Datum) + sizeof(bool)));
7015			values = (Datum *) chunk;
7016			nulls = (bool *) (chunk + td_natts * sizeof(Datum));
7017		}
7018
7019		heap_deform_tuple(tup, tupdesc, values, nulls);
7020
7021		exec_move_row_from_fields(estate, target, newerh,
7022								  values, nulls, tupdesc);
7023	}
7024	else
7025	{
7026		/*
7027		 * Assign all-nulls.
7028		 */
7029		exec_move_row_from_fields(estate, target, newerh,
7030								  NULL, NULL, NULL);
7031	}
7032}
7033
7034/*
7035 * Verify that a PLpgSQL_rec's rectypeid is up-to-date.
7036 */
7037static void
7038revalidate_rectypeid(PLpgSQL_rec *rec)
7039{
7040	PLpgSQL_type *typ = rec->datatype;
7041	TypeCacheEntry *typentry;
7042
7043	if (rec->rectypeid == RECORDOID)
7044		return;					/* it's RECORD, so nothing to do */
7045	Assert(typ != NULL);
7046	if (typ->tcache &&
7047		typ->tcache->tupDesc_identifier == typ->tupdesc_id)
7048	{
7049		/*
7050		 * Although *typ is known up-to-date, it's possible that rectypeid
7051		 * isn't, because *rec is cloned during each function startup from a
7052		 * copy that we don't have a good way to update.  Hence, forcibly fix
7053		 * rectypeid before returning.
7054		 */
7055		rec->rectypeid = typ->typoid;
7056		return;
7057	}
7058
7059	/*
7060	 * typcache entry has suffered invalidation, so re-look-up the type name
7061	 * if possible, and then recheck the type OID.  If we don't have a
7062	 * TypeName, then we just have to soldier on with the OID we've got.
7063	 */
7064	if (typ->origtypname != NULL)
7065	{
7066		/* this bit should match parse_datatype() in pl_gram.y */
7067		typenameTypeIdAndMod(NULL, typ->origtypname,
7068							 &typ->typoid,
7069							 &typ->atttypmod);
7070	}
7071
7072	/* this bit should match build_datatype() in pl_comp.c */
7073	typentry = lookup_type_cache(typ->typoid,
7074								 TYPECACHE_TUPDESC |
7075								 TYPECACHE_DOMAIN_BASE_INFO);
7076	if (typentry->typtype == TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
7077		typentry = lookup_type_cache(typentry->domainBaseType,
7078									 TYPECACHE_TUPDESC);
7079	if (typentry->tupDesc == NULL)
7080	{
7081		/*
7082		 * If we get here, user tried to replace a composite type with a
7083		 * non-composite one.  We're not gonna support that.
7084		 */
7085		ereport(ERROR,
7086				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
7087				 errmsg("type %s is not composite",
7088						format_type_be(typ->typoid))));
7089	}
7090
7091	/*
7092	 * Update tcache and tupdesc_id.  Since we don't support changing to a
7093	 * non-composite type, none of the rest of *typ needs to change.
7094	 */
7095	typ->tcache = typentry;
7096	typ->tupdesc_id = typentry->tupDesc_identifier;
7097
7098	/*
7099	 * Update *rec, too.  (We'll deal with subsidiary RECFIELDs as needed.)
7100	 */
7101	rec->rectypeid = typ->typoid;
7102}
7103
7104/*
7105 * Build an expanded record object suitable for assignment to "rec".
7106 *
7107 * Caller must supply either a source tuple descriptor or a source expanded
7108 * record (not both).  If the record variable has declared type RECORD,
7109 * it'll adopt the source's rowtype.  Even if it doesn't, we may be able to
7110 * piggyback on a source expanded record to save a typcache lookup.
7111 *
7112 * Caller must fill the object with data, then do assign_record_var().
7113 *
7114 * The new record is initially put into the mcontext, so it will be cleaned up
7115 * if we fail before reaching assign_record_var().
7116 */
7117static ExpandedRecordHeader *
7118make_expanded_record_for_rec(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
7119							 PLpgSQL_rec *rec,
7120							 TupleDesc srctupdesc,
7121							 ExpandedRecordHeader *srcerh)
7122{
7123	ExpandedRecordHeader *newerh;
7124	MemoryContext mcontext = get_eval_mcontext(estate);
7125
7126	if (rec->rectypeid != RECORDOID)
7127	{
7128		/*
7129		 * Make sure rec->rectypeid is up-to-date before using it.
7130		 */
7131		revalidate_rectypeid(rec);
7132
7133		/*
7134		 * New record must be of desired type, but maybe srcerh has already
7135		 * done all the same lookups.
7136		 */
7137		if (srcerh && rec->rectypeid == srcerh->er_decltypeid)
7138			newerh = make_expanded_record_from_exprecord(srcerh,
7139														 mcontext);
7140		else
7141			newerh = make_expanded_record_from_typeid(rec->rectypeid, -1,
7142													  mcontext);
7143	}
7144	else
7145	{
7146		/*
7147		 * We'll adopt the input tupdesc.  We can still use
7148		 * make_expanded_record_from_exprecord, if srcerh isn't a composite
7149		 * domain.  (If it is, we effectively adopt its base type.)
7150		 */
7151		if (srcerh && !ExpandedRecordIsDomain(srcerh))
7152			newerh = make_expanded_record_from_exprecord(srcerh,
7153														 mcontext);
7154		else
7155		{
7156			if (!srctupdesc)
7157				srctupdesc = expanded_record_get_tupdesc(srcerh);
7158			newerh = make_expanded_record_from_tupdesc(srctupdesc,
7159													   mcontext);
7160		}
7161	}
7162
7163	return newerh;
7164}
7165
7166/*
7167 * exec_move_row_from_fields	Move arrays of field values into a record or row
7168 *
7169 * When assigning to a record, the caller must have already created a suitable
7170 * new expanded record object, newerh.  Pass NULL when assigning to a row.
7171 *
7172 * tupdesc describes the input row, which might have different column
7173 * types and/or different dropped-column positions than the target.
7174 * values/nulls/tupdesc can all be NULL if we just want to assign nulls to
7175 * all fields of the record or row.
7176 *
7177 * Since this uses the mcontext for workspace, caller should eventually call
7178 * exec_eval_cleanup to prevent long-term memory leaks.
7179 */
7180static void
7181exec_move_row_from_fields(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
7182						  PLpgSQL_variable *target,
7183						  ExpandedRecordHeader *newerh,
7184						  Datum *values, bool *nulls,
7185						  TupleDesc tupdesc)
7186{
7187	int			td_natts = tupdesc ? tupdesc->natts : 0;
7188	int			fnum;
7189	int			anum;
7190	int			strict_multiassignment_level = 0;
7191
7192	/*
7193	 * The extra check strict strict_multi_assignment can be active, only when
7194	 * input tupdesc is specified.
7195	 */
7196	if (tupdesc != NULL)
7197	{
7198		if (plpgsql_extra_errors & PLPGSQL_XCHECK_STRICTMULTIASSIGNMENT)
7199			strict_multiassignment_level = ERROR;
7200		else if (plpgsql_extra_warnings & PLPGSQL_XCHECK_STRICTMULTIASSIGNMENT)
7201			strict_multiassignment_level = WARNING;
7202	}
7203
7204	/* Handle RECORD-target case */
7205	if (target->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC)
7206	{
7207		PLpgSQL_rec *rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) target;
7208		TupleDesc	var_tupdesc;
7209		Datum		newvalues_local[64];
7210		bool		newnulls_local[64];
7211
7212		Assert(newerh != NULL); /* caller must have built new object */
7213
7214		var_tupdesc = expanded_record_get_tupdesc(newerh);
7215
7216		/*
7217		 * Coerce field values if needed.  This might involve dealing with
7218		 * different sets of dropped columns and/or coercing individual column
7219		 * types.  That's sort of a pain, but historically plpgsql has allowed
7220		 * it, so we preserve the behavior.  However, it's worth a quick check
7221		 * to see if the tupdescs are identical.  (Since expandedrecord.c
7222		 * prefers to use refcounted tupdescs from the typcache, expanded
7223		 * records with the same rowtype will have pointer-equal tupdescs.)
7224		 */
7225		if (var_tupdesc != tupdesc)
7226		{
7227			int			vtd_natts = var_tupdesc->natts;
7228			Datum	   *newvalues;
7229			bool	   *newnulls;
7230
7231			/*
7232			 * Need workspace arrays.  If vtd_natts is small enough, use local
7233			 * arrays to save doing a palloc.  Even if it's not small, we can
7234			 * allocate both the Datum and isnull arrays in one palloc chunk.
7235			 */
7236			if (vtd_natts <= lengthof(newvalues_local))
7237			{
7238				newvalues = newvalues_local;
7239				newnulls = newnulls_local;
7240			}
7241			else
7242			{
7243				char	   *chunk;
7244
7245				chunk = eval_mcontext_alloc(estate,
7246											vtd_natts * (sizeof(Datum) + sizeof(bool)));
7247				newvalues = (Datum *) chunk;
7248				newnulls = (bool *) (chunk + vtd_natts * sizeof(Datum));
7249			}
7250
7251			/* Walk over destination columns */
7252			anum = 0;
7253			for (fnum = 0; fnum < vtd_natts; fnum++)
7254			{
7255				Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(var_tupdesc, fnum);
7256				Datum		value;
7257				bool		isnull;
7258				Oid			valtype;
7259				int32		valtypmod;
7260
7261				if (attr->attisdropped)
7262				{
7263					/* expanded_record_set_fields should ignore this column */
7264					continue;	/* skip dropped column in record */
7265				}
7266
7267				while (anum < td_natts &&
7268					   TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, anum)->attisdropped)
7269					anum++;		/* skip dropped column in tuple */
7270
7271				if (anum < td_natts)
7272				{
7273					value = values[anum];
7274					isnull = nulls[anum];
7275					valtype = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, anum)->atttypid;
7276					valtypmod = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, anum)->atttypmod;
7277					anum++;
7278				}
7279				else
7280				{
7281					/* no source for destination column */
7282					value = (Datum) 0;
7283					isnull = true;
7284					valtype = UNKNOWNOID;
7285					valtypmod = -1;
7286
7287					/* When source value is missing */
7288					if (strict_multiassignment_level)
7289						ereport(strict_multiassignment_level,
7290								(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
7291								 errmsg("number of source and target fields in assignment does not match"),
7292						/* translator: %s represents a name of an extra check */
7293								 errdetail("%s check of %s is active.",
7294										   "strict_multi_assignment",
7295										   strict_multiassignment_level == ERROR ? "extra_errors" :
7296										   "extra_warnings"),
7297								 errhint("Make sure the query returns the exact list of columns.")));
7298				}
7299
7300				/* Cast the new value to the right type, if needed. */
7301				newvalues[fnum] = exec_cast_value(estate,
7302												  value,
7303												  &isnull,
7304												  valtype,
7305												  valtypmod,
7306												  attr->atttypid,
7307												  attr->atttypmod);
7308				newnulls[fnum] = isnull;
7309			}
7310
7311			/*
7312			 * When strict_multiassignment extra check is active, then ensure
7313			 * there are no unassigned source attributes.
7314			 */
7315			if (strict_multiassignment_level && anum < td_natts)
7316			{
7317				/* skip dropped columns in the source descriptor */
7318				while (anum < td_natts &&
7319					   TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, anum)->attisdropped)
7320					anum++;
7321
7322				if (anum < td_natts)
7323					ereport(strict_multiassignment_level,
7324							(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
7325							 errmsg("number of source and target fields in assignment does not match"),
7326					/* translator: %s represents a name of an extra check */
7327							 errdetail("%s check of %s is active.",
7328									   "strict_multi_assignment",
7329									   strict_multiassignment_level == ERROR ? "extra_errors" :
7330									   "extra_warnings"),
7331							 errhint("Make sure the query returns the exact list of columns.")));
7332			}
7333
7334			values = newvalues;
7335			nulls = newnulls;
7336		}
7337
7338		/* Insert the coerced field values into the new expanded record */
7339		expanded_record_set_fields(newerh, values, nulls, !estate->atomic);
7340
7341		/* Complete the assignment */
7342		assign_record_var(estate, rec, newerh);
7343
7344		return;
7345	}
7346
7347	/* newerh should not have been passed in non-RECORD cases */
7348	Assert(newerh == NULL);
7349
7350	/*
7351	 * For a row, we assign the individual field values to the variables the
7352	 * row points to.
7353	 *
7354	 * NOTE: both this code and the record code above silently ignore extra
7355	 * columns in the source and assume NULL for missing columns.  This is
7356	 * pretty dubious but it's the historical behavior.
7357	 *
7358	 * If we have no input data at all, we'll assign NULL to all columns of
7359	 * the row variable.
7360	 */
7361	if (target->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_ROW)
7362	{
7363		PLpgSQL_row *row = (PLpgSQL_row *) target;
7364
7365		anum = 0;
7366		for (fnum = 0; fnum < row->nfields; fnum++)
7367		{
7368			PLpgSQL_var *var;
7369			Datum		value;
7370			bool		isnull;
7371			Oid			valtype;
7372			int32		valtypmod;
7373
7374			var = (PLpgSQL_var *) (estate->datums[row->varnos[fnum]]);
7375
7376			while (anum < td_natts &&
7377				   TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, anum)->attisdropped)
7378				anum++;			/* skip dropped column in tuple */
7379
7380			if (anum < td_natts)
7381			{
7382				value = values[anum];
7383				isnull = nulls[anum];
7384				valtype = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, anum)->atttypid;
7385				valtypmod = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, anum)->atttypmod;
7386				anum++;
7387			}
7388			else
7389			{
7390				/* no source for destination column */
7391				value = (Datum) 0;
7392				isnull = true;
7393				valtype = UNKNOWNOID;
7394				valtypmod = -1;
7395
7396				if (strict_multiassignment_level)
7397					ereport(strict_multiassignment_level,
7398							(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
7399							 errmsg("number of source and target fields in assignment does not match"),
7400					/* translator: %s represents a name of an extra check */
7401							 errdetail("%s check of %s is active.",
7402									   "strict_multi_assignment",
7403									   strict_multiassignment_level == ERROR ? "extra_errors" :
7404									   "extra_warnings"),
7405							 errhint("Make sure the query returns the exact list of columns.")));
7406			}
7407
7408			exec_assign_value(estate, (PLpgSQL_datum *) var,
7409							  value, isnull, valtype, valtypmod);
7410		}
7411
7412		/*
7413		 * When strict_multiassignment extra check is active, ensure there are
7414		 * no unassigned source attributes.
7415		 */
7416		if (strict_multiassignment_level && anum < td_natts)
7417		{
7418			while (anum < td_natts &&
7419				   TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, anum)->attisdropped)
7420				anum++;			/* skip dropped column in tuple */
7421
7422			if (anum < td_natts)
7423				ereport(strict_multiassignment_level,
7424						(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
7425						 errmsg("number of source and target fields in assignment does not match"),
7426				/* translator: %s represents a name of an extra check */
7427						 errdetail("%s check of %s is active.",
7428								   "strict_multi_assignment",
7429								   strict_multiassignment_level == ERROR ? "extra_errors" :
7430								   "extra_warnings"),
7431						 errhint("Make sure the query returns the exact list of columns.")));
7432		}
7433
7434		return;
7435	}
7436
7437	elog(ERROR, "unsupported target type: %d", target->dtype);
7438}
7439
7440/*
7441 * compatible_tupdescs: detect whether two tupdescs are physically compatible
7442 *
7443 * TRUE indicates that a tuple satisfying src_tupdesc can be used directly as
7444 * a value for a composite variable using dst_tupdesc.
7445 */
7446static bool
7447compatible_tupdescs(TupleDesc src_tupdesc, TupleDesc dst_tupdesc)
7448{
7449	int			i;
7450
7451	/* Possibly we could allow src_tupdesc to have extra columns? */
7452	if (dst_tupdesc->natts != src_tupdesc->natts)
7453		return false;
7454
7455	for (i = 0; i < dst_tupdesc->natts; i++)
7456	{
7457		Form_pg_attribute dattr = TupleDescAttr(dst_tupdesc, i);
7458		Form_pg_attribute sattr = TupleDescAttr(src_tupdesc, i);
7459
7460		if (dattr->attisdropped != sattr->attisdropped)
7461			return false;
7462		if (!dattr->attisdropped)
7463		{
7464			/* Normal columns must match by type and typmod */
7465			if (dattr->atttypid != sattr->atttypid ||
7466				(dattr->atttypmod >= 0 &&
7467				 dattr->atttypmod != sattr->atttypmod))
7468				return false;
7469		}
7470		else
7471		{
7472			/* Dropped columns are OK as long as length/alignment match */
7473			if (dattr->attlen != sattr->attlen ||
7474				dattr->attalign != sattr->attalign)
7475				return false;
7476		}
7477	}
7478	return true;
7479}
7480
7481/* ----------
7482 * make_tuple_from_row		Make a tuple from the values of a row object
7483 *
7484 * A NULL return indicates rowtype mismatch; caller must raise suitable error
7485 *
7486 * The result tuple is freshly palloc'd in caller's context.  Some junk
7487 * may be left behind in eval_mcontext, too.
7488 * ----------
7489 */
7490static HeapTuple
7491make_tuple_from_row(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
7492					PLpgSQL_row *row,
7493					TupleDesc tupdesc)
7494{
7495	int			natts = tupdesc->natts;
7496	HeapTuple	tuple;
7497	Datum	   *dvalues;
7498	bool	   *nulls;
7499	int			i;
7500
7501	if (natts != row->nfields)
7502		return NULL;
7503
7504	dvalues = (Datum *) eval_mcontext_alloc0(estate, natts * sizeof(Datum));
7505	nulls = (bool *) eval_mcontext_alloc(estate, natts * sizeof(bool));
7506
7507	for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
7508	{
7509		Oid			fieldtypeid;
7510		int32		fieldtypmod;
7511
7512		if (TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i)->attisdropped)
7513		{
7514			nulls[i] = true;	/* leave the column as null */
7515			continue;
7516		}
7517
7518		exec_eval_datum(estate, estate->datums[row->varnos[i]],
7519						&fieldtypeid, &fieldtypmod,
7520						&dvalues[i], &nulls[i]);
7521		if (fieldtypeid != TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i)->atttypid)
7522			return NULL;
7523		/* XXX should we insist on typmod match, too? */
7524	}
7525
7526	tuple = heap_form_tuple(tupdesc, dvalues, nulls);
7527
7528	return tuple;
7529}
7530
7531/*
7532 * deconstruct_composite_datum		extract tuple+tupdesc from composite Datum
7533 *
7534 * The caller must supply a HeapTupleData variable, in which we set up a
7535 * tuple header pointing to the composite datum's body.  To make the tuple
7536 * value outlive that variable, caller would need to apply heap_copytuple...
7537 * but current callers only need a short-lived tuple value anyway.
7538 *
7539 * Returns a pointer to the TupleDesc of the datum's rowtype.
7540 * Caller is responsible for calling ReleaseTupleDesc when done with it.
7541 *
7542 * Note: it's caller's responsibility to be sure value is of composite type.
7543 * Also, best to call this in a short-lived context, as it might leak memory.
7544 */
7545static TupleDesc
7546deconstruct_composite_datum(Datum value, HeapTupleData *tmptup)
7547{
7548	HeapTupleHeader td;
7549	Oid			tupType;
7550	int32		tupTypmod;
7551
