7552	/* Get tuple body (note this could involve detoasting) */
7553	td = DatumGetHeapTupleHeader(value);
7554
7555	/* Build a temporary HeapTuple control structure */
7556	tmptup->t_len = HeapTupleHeaderGetDatumLength(td);
7557	ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(tmptup->t_self));
7558	tmptup->t_tableOid = InvalidOid;
7559	tmptup->t_data = td;
7560
7561	/* Extract rowtype info and find a tupdesc */
7562	tupType = HeapTupleHeaderGetTypeId(td);
7563	tupTypmod = HeapTupleHeaderGetTypMod(td);
7564	return lookup_rowtype_tupdesc(tupType, tupTypmod);
7565}
7566
7567/*
7568 * exec_move_row_from_datum		Move a composite Datum into a record or row
7569 *
7570 * This is equivalent to deconstruct_composite_datum() followed by
7571 * exec_move_row(), but we can optimize things if the Datum is an
7572 * expanded-record reference.
7573 *
7574 * Note: it's caller's responsibility to be sure value is of composite type.
7575 */
7576static void
7577exec_move_row_from_datum(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
7578						 PLpgSQL_variable *target,
7579						 Datum value)
7580{
7581	/* Check to see if source is an expanded record */
7582	if (VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL_EXPANDED(DatumGetPointer(value)))
7583	{
7584		ExpandedRecordHeader *erh = (ExpandedRecordHeader *) DatumGetEOHP(value);
7585		ExpandedRecordHeader *newerh = NULL;
7586
7587		Assert(erh->er_magic == ER_MAGIC);
7588
7589		/* These cases apply if the target is record not row... */
7590		if (target->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC)
7591		{
7592			PLpgSQL_rec *rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) target;
7593
7594			/*
7595			 * If it's the same record already stored in the variable, do
7596			 * nothing.  This would happen only in silly cases like "r := r",
7597			 * but we need some check to avoid possibly freeing the variable's
7598			 * live value below.  Note that this applies even if what we have
7599			 * is a R/O pointer.
7600			 */
7601			if (erh == rec->erh)
7602				return;
7603
7604			/*
7605			 * Make sure rec->rectypeid is up-to-date before using it.
7606			 */
7607			revalidate_rectypeid(rec);
7608
7609			/*
7610			 * If we have a R/W pointer, we're allowed to just commandeer
7611			 * ownership of the expanded record.  If it's of the right type to
7612			 * put into the record variable, do that.  (Note we don't accept
7613			 * an expanded record of a composite-domain type as a RECORD
7614			 * value.  We'll treat it as the base composite type instead;
7615			 * compare logic in make_expanded_record_for_rec.)
7616			 */
7617			if (VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL_EXPANDED_RW(DatumGetPointer(value)) &&
7618				(rec->rectypeid == erh->er_decltypeid ||
7619				 (rec->rectypeid == RECORDOID &&
7620				  !ExpandedRecordIsDomain(erh))))
7621			{
7622				assign_record_var(estate, rec, erh);
7623				return;
7624			}
7625
7626			/*
7627			 * If we already have an expanded record object in the target
7628			 * variable, and the source record contains a valid tuple
7629			 * representation with the right rowtype, then we can skip making
7630			 * a new expanded record and just assign the tuple with
7631			 * expanded_record_set_tuple.  (We can't do the equivalent if we
7632			 * have to do field-by-field assignment, since that wouldn't be
7633			 * atomic if there's an error.)  We consider that there's a
7634			 * rowtype match only if it's the same named composite type or
7635			 * same registered rowtype; checking for matches of anonymous
7636			 * rowtypes would be more expensive than this is worth.
7637			 */
7638			if (rec->erh &&
7639				(erh->flags & ER_FLAG_FVALUE_VALID) &&
7640				erh->er_typeid == rec->erh->er_typeid &&
7641				(erh->er_typeid != RECORDOID ||
7642				 (erh->er_typmod == rec->erh->er_typmod &&
7643				  erh->er_typmod >= 0)))
7644			{
7645				expanded_record_set_tuple(rec->erh, erh->fvalue,
7646										  true, !estate->atomic);
7647				return;
7648			}
7649
7650			/*
7651			 * Otherwise we're gonna need a new expanded record object.  Make
7652			 * it here in hopes of piggybacking on the source object's
7653			 * previous typcache lookup.
7654			 */
7655			newerh = make_expanded_record_for_rec(estate, rec, NULL, erh);
7656
7657			/*
7658			 * If the expanded record contains a valid tuple representation,
7659			 * and we don't need rowtype conversion, then just copying the
7660			 * tuple is probably faster than field-by-field processing.  (This
7661			 * isn't duplicative of the previous check, since here we will
7662			 * catch the case where the record variable was previously empty.)
7663			 */
7664			if ((erh->flags & ER_FLAG_FVALUE_VALID) &&
7665				(rec->rectypeid == RECORDOID ||
7666				 rec->rectypeid == erh->er_typeid))
7667			{
7668				expanded_record_set_tuple(newerh, erh->fvalue,
7669										  true, !estate->atomic);
7670				assign_record_var(estate, rec, newerh);
7671				return;
7672			}
7673
7674			/*
7675			 * Need to special-case empty source record, else code below would
7676			 * leak newerh.
7677			 */
7678			if (ExpandedRecordIsEmpty(erh))
7679			{
7680				/* Set newerh to a row of NULLs */
7681				deconstruct_expanded_record(newerh);
7682				assign_record_var(estate, rec, newerh);
7683				return;
7684			}
7685		}						/* end of record-target-only cases */
7686
7687		/*
7688		 * If the source expanded record is empty, we should treat that like a
7689		 * NULL tuple value.  (We're unlikely to see such a case, but we must
7690		 * check this; deconstruct_expanded_record would cause a change of
7691		 * logical state, which is not OK.)
7692		 */
7693		if (ExpandedRecordIsEmpty(erh))
7694		{
7695			exec_move_row(estate, target, NULL,
7696						  expanded_record_get_tupdesc(erh));
7697			return;
7698		}
7699
7700		/*
7701		 * Otherwise, ensure that the source record is deconstructed, and
7702		 * assign from its field values.
7703		 */
7704		deconstruct_expanded_record(erh);
7705		exec_move_row_from_fields(estate, target, newerh,
7706								  erh->dvalues, erh->dnulls,
7707								  expanded_record_get_tupdesc(erh));
7708	}
7709	else
7710	{
7711		/*
7712		 * Nope, we've got a plain composite Datum.  Deconstruct it; but we
7713		 * don't use deconstruct_composite_datum(), because we may be able to
7714		 * skip calling lookup_rowtype_tupdesc().
7715		 */
7716		HeapTupleHeader td;
7717		HeapTupleData tmptup;
7718		Oid			tupType;
7719		int32		tupTypmod;
7720		TupleDesc	tupdesc;
7721		MemoryContext oldcontext;
7722
7723		/* Ensure that any detoasted data winds up in the eval_mcontext */
7724		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
7725		/* Get tuple body (note this could involve detoasting) */
7726		td = DatumGetHeapTupleHeader(value);
7727		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
7728
7729		/* Build a temporary HeapTuple control structure */
7730		tmptup.t_len = HeapTupleHeaderGetDatumLength(td);
7731		ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(tmptup.t_self));
7732		tmptup.t_tableOid = InvalidOid;
7733		tmptup.t_data = td;
7734
7735		/* Extract rowtype info */
7736		tupType = HeapTupleHeaderGetTypeId(td);
7737		tupTypmod = HeapTupleHeaderGetTypMod(td);
7738
7739		/* Now, if the target is record not row, maybe we can optimize ... */
7740		if (target->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC)
7741		{
7742			PLpgSQL_rec *rec = (PLpgSQL_rec *) target;
7743
7744			/*
7745			 * If we already have an expanded record object in the target
7746			 * variable, and the source datum has a matching rowtype, then we
7747			 * can skip making a new expanded record and just assign the tuple
7748			 * with expanded_record_set_tuple.  We consider that there's a
7749			 * rowtype match only if it's the same named composite type or
7750			 * same registered rowtype.  (Checking to reject an anonymous
7751			 * rowtype here should be redundant, but let's be safe.)
7752			 */
7753			if (rec->erh &&
7754				tupType == rec->erh->er_typeid &&
7755				(tupType != RECORDOID ||
7756				 (tupTypmod == rec->erh->er_typmod &&
7757				  tupTypmod >= 0)))
7758			{
7759				expanded_record_set_tuple(rec->erh, &tmptup,
7760										  true, !estate->atomic);
7761				return;
7762			}
7763
7764			/*
7765			 * If the source datum has a rowtype compatible with the target
7766			 * variable, just build a new expanded record and assign the tuple
7767			 * into it.  Using make_expanded_record_from_typeid() here saves
7768			 * one typcache lookup compared to the code below.
7769			 */
7770			if (rec->rectypeid == RECORDOID || rec->rectypeid == tupType)
7771			{
7772				ExpandedRecordHeader *newerh;
7773				MemoryContext mcontext = get_eval_mcontext(estate);
7774
7775				newerh = make_expanded_record_from_typeid(tupType, tupTypmod,
7776														  mcontext);
7777				expanded_record_set_tuple(newerh, &tmptup,
7778										  true, !estate->atomic);
7779				assign_record_var(estate, rec, newerh);
7780				return;
7781			}
7782
7783			/*
7784			 * Otherwise, we're going to need conversion, so fall through to
7785			 * do it the hard way.
7786			 */
7787		}
7788
7789		/*
7790		 * ROW target, or unoptimizable RECORD target, so we have to expend a
7791		 * lookup to obtain the source datum's tupdesc.
7792		 */
7793		tupdesc = lookup_rowtype_tupdesc(tupType, tupTypmod);
7794
7795		/* Do the move */
7796		exec_move_row(estate, target, &tmptup, tupdesc);
7797
7798		/* Release tupdesc usage count */
7799		ReleaseTupleDesc(tupdesc);
7800	}
7801}
7802
7803/*
7804 * If we have not created an expanded record to hold the record variable's
7805 * value, do so.  The expanded record will be "empty", so this does not
7806 * change the logical state of the record variable: it's still NULL.
7807 * However, now we'll have a tupdesc with which we can e.g. look up fields.
7808 */
7809static void
7810instantiate_empty_record_variable(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, PLpgSQL_rec *rec)
7811{
7812	Assert(rec->erh == NULL);	/* else caller error */
7813
7814	/* If declared type is RECORD, we can't instantiate */
7815	if (rec->rectypeid == RECORDOID)
7816		ereport(ERROR,
7817				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
7818				 errmsg("record \"%s\" is not assigned yet", rec->refname),
7819				 errdetail("The tuple structure of a not-yet-assigned record is indeterminate.")));
7820
7821	/* Make sure rec->rectypeid is up-to-date before using it */
7822	revalidate_rectypeid(rec);
7823
7824	/* OK, do it */
7825	rec->erh = make_expanded_record_from_typeid(rec->rectypeid, -1,
7826												estate->datum_context);
7827}
7828
7829/* ----------
7830 * convert_value_to_string			Convert a non-null Datum to C string
7831 *
7832 * Note: the result is in the estate's eval_mcontext, and will be cleared
7833 * by the next exec_eval_cleanup() call.  The invoked output function might
7834 * leave additional cruft there as well, so just pfree'ing the result string
7835 * would not be enough to avoid memory leaks if we did not do it like this.
7836 * In most usages the Datum being passed in is also in that context (if
7837 * pass-by-reference) and so an exec_eval_cleanup() call is needed anyway.
7838 *
7839 * Note: not caching the conversion function lookup is bad for performance.
7840 * However, this function isn't currently used in any places where an extra
7841 * catalog lookup or two seems like a big deal.
7842 * ----------
7843 */
7844static char *
7845convert_value_to_string(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, Datum value, Oid valtype)
7846{
7847	char	   *result;
7848	MemoryContext oldcontext;
7849	Oid			typoutput;
7850	bool		typIsVarlena;
7851
7852	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
7853	getTypeOutputInfo(valtype, &typoutput, &typIsVarlena);
7854	result = OidOutputFunctionCall(typoutput, value);
7855	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
7856
7857	return result;
7858}
7859
7860/* ----------
7861 * exec_cast_value			Cast a value if required
7862 *
7863 * Note that *isnull is an input and also an output parameter.  While it's
7864 * unlikely that a cast operation would produce null from non-null or vice
7865 * versa, that could happen in principle.
7866 *
7867 * Note: the estate's eval_mcontext is used for temporary storage, and may
7868 * also contain the result Datum if we have to do a conversion to a pass-
7869 * by-reference data type.  Be sure to do an exec_eval_cleanup() call when
7870 * done with the result.
7871 * ----------
7872 */
7873static inline Datum
7874exec_cast_value(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
7875				Datum value, bool *isnull,
7876				Oid valtype, int32 valtypmod,
7877				Oid reqtype, int32 reqtypmod)
7878{
7879	/*
7880	 * If the type of the given value isn't what's requested, convert it.
7881	 */
7882	if (valtype != reqtype ||
7883		(valtypmod != reqtypmod && reqtypmod != -1))
7884	{
7885		/* We keep the slow path out-of-line. */
7886		value = do_cast_value(estate, value, isnull, valtype, valtypmod,
7887							  reqtype, reqtypmod);
7888	}
7889
7890	return value;
7891}
7892
7893/* ----------
7894 * do_cast_value			Slow path for exec_cast_value.
7895 * ----------
7896 */
7897static Datum
7898do_cast_value(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
7899			  Datum value, bool *isnull,
7900			  Oid valtype, int32 valtypmod,
7901			  Oid reqtype, int32 reqtypmod)
7902{
7903	plpgsql_CastHashEntry *cast_entry;
7904
7905	cast_entry = get_cast_hashentry(estate,
7906									valtype, valtypmod,
7907									reqtype, reqtypmod);
7908	if (cast_entry)
7909	{
7910		ExprContext *econtext = estate->eval_econtext;
7911		MemoryContext oldcontext;
7912
7913		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
7914
7915		econtext->caseValue_datum = value;
7916		econtext->caseValue_isNull = *isnull;
7917
7918		cast_entry->cast_in_use = true;
7919
7920		value = ExecEvalExpr(cast_entry->cast_exprstate, econtext,
7921							 isnull);
7922
7923		cast_entry->cast_in_use = false;
7924
7925		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
7926	}
7927
7928	return value;
7929}
7930
7931/* ----------
7932 * get_cast_hashentry			Look up how to perform a type cast
7933 *
7934 * Returns a plpgsql_CastHashEntry if an expression has to be evaluated,
7935 * or NULL if the cast is a mere no-op relabeling.  If there's work to be
7936 * done, the cast_exprstate field contains an expression evaluation tree
7937 * based on a CaseTestExpr input, and the cast_in_use field should be set
7938 * true while executing it.
7939 * ----------
7940 */
7941static plpgsql_CastHashEntry *
7942get_cast_hashentry(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
7943				   Oid srctype, int32 srctypmod,
7944				   Oid dsttype, int32 dsttypmod)
7945{
7946	plpgsql_CastHashKey cast_key;
7947	plpgsql_CastHashEntry *cast_entry;
7948	plpgsql_CastExprHashEntry *expr_entry;
7949	bool		found;
7950	LocalTransactionId curlxid;
7951	MemoryContext oldcontext;
7952
7953	/* Look for existing entry */
7954	cast_key.srctype = srctype;
7955	cast_key.dsttype = dsttype;
7956	cast_key.srctypmod = srctypmod;
7957	cast_key.dsttypmod = dsttypmod;
7958	cast_entry = (plpgsql_CastHashEntry *) hash_search(estate->cast_hash,
7959													   &cast_key,
7960													   HASH_ENTER, &found);
7961	if (!found)					/* initialize if new entry */
7962	{
7963		/* We need a second lookup to see if a cast_expr_hash entry exists */
7964		expr_entry = (plpgsql_CastExprHashEntry *) hash_search(cast_expr_hash,
7965															   &cast_key,
7966															   HASH_ENTER,
7967															   &found);
7968		if (!found)				/* initialize if new expr entry */
7969			expr_entry->cast_cexpr = NULL;
7970
7971		cast_entry->cast_centry = expr_entry;
7972		cast_entry->cast_exprstate = NULL;
7973		cast_entry->cast_in_use = false;
7974		cast_entry->cast_lxid = InvalidLocalTransactionId;
7975	}
7976	else
7977	{
7978		/* Use always-valid link to avoid a second hash lookup */
7979		expr_entry = cast_entry->cast_centry;
7980	}
7981
7982	if (expr_entry->cast_cexpr == NULL ||
7983		!expr_entry->cast_cexpr->is_valid)
7984	{
7985		/*
7986		 * We've not looked up this coercion before, or we have but the cached
7987		 * expression has been invalidated.
7988		 */
7989		Node	   *cast_expr;
7990		CachedExpression *cast_cexpr;
7991		CaseTestExpr *placeholder;
7992
7993		/*
7994		 * Drop old cached expression if there is one.
7995		 */
7996		if (expr_entry->cast_cexpr)
7997		{
7998			FreeCachedExpression(expr_entry->cast_cexpr);
7999			expr_entry->cast_cexpr = NULL;
8000		}
8001
8002		/*
8003		 * Since we could easily fail (no such coercion), construct a
8004		 * temporary coercion expression tree in the short-lived
8005		 * eval_mcontext, then if successful save it as a CachedExpression.
8006		 */
8007		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
8008
8009		/*
8010		 * We use a CaseTestExpr as the base of the coercion tree, since it's
8011		 * very cheap to insert the source value for that.
8012		 */
8013		placeholder = makeNode(CaseTestExpr);
8014		placeholder->typeId = srctype;
8015		placeholder->typeMod = srctypmod;
8016		placeholder->collation = get_typcollation(srctype);
8017
8018		/*
8019		 * Apply coercion.  We use the special coercion context
8020		 * COERCION_PLPGSQL to match plpgsql's historical behavior, namely
8021		 * that any cast not available at ASSIGNMENT level will be implemented
8022		 * as an I/O coercion.  (It's somewhat dubious that we prefer I/O
8023		 * coercion over cast pathways that exist at EXPLICIT level.  Changing
8024		 * that would cause assorted minor behavioral differences though, and
8025		 * a user who wants the explicit-cast behavior can always write an
8026		 * explicit cast.)
8027		 *
8028		 * If source type is UNKNOWN, coerce_to_target_type will fail (it only
8029		 * expects to see that for Const input nodes), so don't call it; we'll
8030		 * apply CoerceViaIO instead.  Likewise, it doesn't currently work for
8031		 * coercing RECORD to some other type, so skip for that too.
8032		 */
8033		if (srctype == UNKNOWNOID || srctype == RECORDOID)
8034			cast_expr = NULL;
8035		else
8036			cast_expr = coerce_to_target_type(NULL,
8037											  (Node *) placeholder, srctype,
8038											  dsttype, dsttypmod,
8039											  COERCION_PLPGSQL,
8040											  COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST,
8041											  -1);
8042
8043		/*
8044		 * If there's no cast path according to the parser, fall back to using
8045		 * an I/O coercion; this is semantically dubious but matches plpgsql's
8046		 * historical behavior.  We would need something of the sort for
8047		 * UNKNOWN literals in any case.  (This is probably now only reachable
8048		 * in the case where srctype is UNKNOWN/RECORD.)
8049		 */
8050		if (cast_expr == NULL)
8051		{
8052			CoerceViaIO *iocoerce = makeNode(CoerceViaIO);
8053
8054			iocoerce->arg = (Expr *) placeholder;
8055			iocoerce->resulttype = dsttype;
8056			iocoerce->resultcollid = InvalidOid;
8057			iocoerce->coerceformat = COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST;
8058			iocoerce->location = -1;
8059			cast_expr = (Node *) iocoerce;
8060			if (dsttypmod != -1)
8061				cast_expr = coerce_to_target_type(NULL,
8062												  cast_expr, dsttype,
8063												  dsttype, dsttypmod,
8064												  COERCION_ASSIGNMENT,
8065												  COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST,
8066												  -1);
8067		}
8068
8069		/* Note: we don't bother labeling the expression tree with collation */
8070
8071		/* Plan the expression and build a CachedExpression */
8072		cast_cexpr = GetCachedExpression(cast_expr);
8073		cast_expr = cast_cexpr->expr;
8074
8075		/* Detect whether we have a no-op (RelabelType) coercion */
8076		if (IsA(cast_expr, RelabelType) &&
8077			((RelabelType *) cast_expr)->arg == (Expr *) placeholder)
8078			cast_expr = NULL;
8079
8080		/* Now we can fill in the expression hashtable entry. */
8081		expr_entry->cast_cexpr = cast_cexpr;
8082		expr_entry->cast_expr = (Expr *) cast_expr;
8083
8084		/* Be sure to reset the exprstate hashtable entry, too. */
8085		cast_entry->cast_exprstate = NULL;
8086		cast_entry->cast_in_use = false;
8087		cast_entry->cast_lxid = InvalidLocalTransactionId;
8088
8089		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
8090	}
8091
8092	/* Done if we have determined that this is a no-op cast. */
8093	if (expr_entry->cast_expr == NULL)
8094		return NULL;
8095
8096	/*
8097	 * Prepare the expression for execution, if it's not been done already in
8098	 * the current transaction; also, if it's marked busy in the current
8099	 * transaction, abandon that expression tree and build a new one, so as to
8100	 * avoid potential problems with recursive cast expressions and failed
8101	 * executions.  (We will leak some memory intra-transaction if that
8102	 * happens a lot, but we don't expect it to.)  It's okay to update the
8103	 * hash table with the new tree because all plpgsql functions within a
8104	 * given transaction share the same simple_eval_estate.  (Well, regular
8105	 * functions do; DO blocks have private simple_eval_estates, and private
8106	 * cast hash tables to go with them.)
8107	 */
8108	curlxid = MyProc->vxid.lxid;
8109	if (cast_entry->cast_lxid != curlxid || cast_entry->cast_in_use)
8110	{
8111		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(estate->simple_eval_estate->es_query_cxt);
8112		cast_entry->cast_exprstate = ExecInitExpr(expr_entry->cast_expr, NULL);
8113		cast_entry->cast_in_use = false;
8114		cast_entry->cast_lxid = curlxid;
8115		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
8116	}
8117
8118	return cast_entry;
8119}
8120
8121
8122/* ----------
8123 * exec_simple_check_plan -		Check if a plan is simple enough to
8124 *								be evaluated by ExecEvalExpr() instead
8125 *								of SPI.
8126 *
8127 * Note: the refcount manipulations in this function assume that expr->plan
8128 * is a "saved" SPI plan.  That's a bit annoying from the caller's standpoint,
8129 * but it's otherwise difficult to avoid leaking the plan on failure.
8130 * ----------
8131 */
8132static void
8133exec_simple_check_plan(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, PLpgSQL_expr *expr)
8134{
8135	List	   *plansources;
8136	CachedPlanSource *plansource;
8137	CachedPlan *cplan;
8138	MemoryContext oldcontext;
8139
8140	/*
8141	 * Initialize to "not simple", and reset R/W optimizability.
8142	 */
8143	expr->expr_simple_expr = NULL;
8144	expr->expr_rwopt = PLPGSQL_RWOPT_UNKNOWN;
8145	expr->expr_rw_param = NULL;
8146
8147	/*
8148	 * Check the analyzed-and-rewritten form of the query to see if we will be
8149	 * able to treat it as a simple expression.  Since this function is only
8150	 * called immediately after creating the CachedPlanSource, we need not
8151	 * worry about the query being stale.
8152	 */
8153	if (!exec_is_simple_query(expr))
8154		return;
8155
8156	/* exec_is_simple_query verified that there's just one CachedPlanSource */
8157	plansources = SPI_plan_get_plan_sources(expr->plan);
8158	plansource = (CachedPlanSource *) linitial(plansources);
8159
8160	/*
8161	 * Get the generic plan for the query.  If replanning is needed, do that
8162	 * work in the eval_mcontext.  (Note that replanning could throw an error,
8163	 * in which case the expr is left marked "not simple", which is fine.)
8164	 */
8165	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
8166	cplan = SPI_plan_get_cached_plan(expr->plan);
8167	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
8168
8169	/* Can't fail, because we checked for a single CachedPlanSource above */
8170	Assert(cplan != NULL);
8171
8172	/*
8173	 * Verify that plancache.c thinks the plan is simple enough to use
8174	 * CachedPlanIsSimplyValid.  Given the restrictions above, it's unlikely
8175	 * that this could fail, but if it does, just treat plan as not simple. On
8176	 * success, save a refcount on the plan in the simple-expression resowner.
8177	 */
8178	if (CachedPlanAllowsSimpleValidityCheck(plansource, cplan,
8179											estate->simple_eval_resowner))
8180	{
8181		/* Remember that we have the refcount */
8182		expr->expr_simple_plansource = plansource;
8183		expr->expr_simple_plan = cplan;
8184		expr->expr_simple_plan_lxid = MyProc->vxid.lxid;
8185
8186		/* Share the remaining work with the replan code path */
8187		exec_save_simple_expr(expr, cplan);
8188	}
8189
8190	/*
8191	 * Release the plan refcount obtained by SPI_plan_get_cached_plan.  (This
8192	 * refcount is held by the wrong resowner, so we can't just repurpose it.)
8193	 */
8194	ReleaseCachedPlan(cplan, CurrentResourceOwner);
8195}
8196
8197/*
8198 * exec_is_simple_query - precheck a query tree to see if it might be simple
8199 *
8200 * Check the analyzed-and-rewritten form of a query to see if we will be
8201 * able to treat it as a simple expression.  It is caller's responsibility
8202 * that the CachedPlanSource be up-to-date.
8203 */
8204static bool
8205exec_is_simple_query(PLpgSQL_expr *expr)
8206{
8207	List	   *plansources;
8208	CachedPlanSource *plansource;
8209	Query	   *query;
8210
8211	/*
8212	 * We can only test queries that resulted in exactly one CachedPlanSource.
8213	 */
8214	plansources = SPI_plan_get_plan_sources(expr->plan);
8215	if (list_length(plansources) != 1)
8216		return false;
8217	plansource = (CachedPlanSource *) linitial(plansources);
8218
8219	/*
8220	 * 1. There must be one single querytree.
8221	 */
8222	if (list_length(plansource->query_list) != 1)
8223		return false;
8224	query = (Query *) linitial(plansource->query_list);
8225
8226	/*
8227	 * 2. It must be a plain SELECT query without any input tables.
8228	 */
8229	if (!IsA(query, Query))
8230		return false;
8231	if (query->commandType != CMD_SELECT)
8232		return false;
8233	if (query->rtable != NIL)
8234		return false;
8235
8236	/*
8237	 * 3. Can't have any subplans, aggregates, qual clauses either.  (These
8238	 * tests should generally match what inline_function() checks before
8239	 * inlining a SQL function; otherwise, inlining could change our
8240	 * conclusion about whether an expression is simple, which we don't want.)
8241	 */
8242	if (query->hasAggs ||
8243		query->hasWindowFuncs ||
8244		query->hasTargetSRFs ||
8245		query->hasSubLinks ||
8246		query->cteList ||
8247		query->jointree->fromlist ||
8248		query->jointree->quals ||
8249		query->groupClause ||
8250		query->groupingSets ||
8251		query->havingQual ||
8252		query->windowClause ||
8253		query->distinctClause ||
8254		query->sortClause ||
8255		query->limitOffset ||
8256		query->limitCount ||
8257		query->setOperations)
8258		return false;
8259
8260	/*
8261	 * 4. The query must have a single attribute as result.
8262	 */
8263	if (list_length(query->targetList) != 1)
8264		return false;
8265
8266	/*
8267	 * OK, we can treat it as a simple plan.
8268	 */
8269	return true;
8270}
8271
8272/*
8273 * exec_save_simple_expr --- extract simple expression from CachedPlan
8274 */
8275static void
8276exec_save_simple_expr(PLpgSQL_expr *expr, CachedPlan *cplan)
8277{
8278	PlannedStmt *stmt;
8279	Plan	   *plan;
8280	Expr	   *tle_expr;
8281
8282	/*
8283	 * Given the checks that exec_simple_check_plan did, none of the Asserts
8284	 * here should ever fail.
8285	 */
8286
8287	/* Extract the single PlannedStmt */
8288	Assert(list_length(cplan->stmt_list) == 1);
8289	stmt = linitial_node(PlannedStmt, cplan->stmt_list);
8290	Assert(stmt->commandType == CMD_SELECT);
8291
8292	/*
8293	 * Ordinarily, the plan node should be a simple Result.  However, if
8294	 * debug_parallel_query is on, the planner might've stuck a Gather node
8295	 * atop that; and/or if this plan is for a scrollable cursor, the planner
8296	 * might've stuck a Material node atop it.  The simplest way to deal with
8297	 * this is to look through the Gather and/or Material nodes.  The upper
8298	 * node's tlist would normally contain a Var referencing the child node's
8299	 * output ... but setrefs.c might also have copied a Const as-is.
8300	 */
8301	plan = stmt->planTree;
8302	for (;;)
8303	{
8304		/* Extract the single tlist expression */
8305		Assert(list_length(plan->targetlist) == 1);
8306		tle_expr = linitial_node(TargetEntry, plan->targetlist)->expr;
8307
8308		if (IsA(plan, Result))
8309		{
8310			Assert(plan->lefttree == NULL &&
8311				   plan->righttree == NULL &&
8312				   plan->initPlan == NULL &&
8313				   plan->qual == NULL &&
8314				   ((Result *) plan)->resconstantqual == NULL);
8315			break;
8316		}
8317		else if (IsA(plan, Gather) || IsA(plan, Material))
8318		{
8319			Assert(plan->lefttree != NULL &&
8320				   plan->righttree == NULL &&
8321				   plan->initPlan == NULL &&
8322				   plan->qual == NULL);
8323			/* If setrefs.c copied up a Const, no need to look further */
8324			if (IsA(tle_expr, Const))
8325				break;
8326			/* Otherwise, it better be an outer Var */
8327			Assert(IsA(tle_expr, Var));
8328			Assert(((Var *) tle_expr)->varno == OUTER_VAR);
8329			/* Descend to the child node */
8330			plan = plan->lefttree;
8331		}
8332		else
8333			elog(ERROR, "unexpected plan node type: %d",
8334				 (int) nodeTag(plan));
8335	}
8336
8337	/*
8338	 * Save the simple expression, and initialize state to "not valid in
8339	 * current transaction".
8340	 */
8341	expr->expr_simple_expr = tle_expr;
8342	expr->expr_simple_state = NULL;
8343	expr->expr_simple_in_use = false;
8344	expr->expr_simple_lxid = InvalidLocalTransactionId;
8345	/* Also stash away the expression result type */
8346	expr->expr_simple_type = exprType((Node *) tle_expr);
8347	expr->expr_simple_typmod = exprTypmod((Node *) tle_expr);
8348	/* We also want to remember if it is immutable or not */
8349	expr->expr_simple_mutable = contain_mutable_functions((Node *) tle_expr);
8350}
8351
8352/*
8353 * exec_check_rw_parameter --- can we pass expanded object as read/write param?
8354 *
8355 * There are two separate cases in which we can optimize an update to a
8356 * variable that has a read/write expanded value by letting the called
8357 * expression operate directly on the expanded value.  In both cases we
8358 * are considering assignments like "var := array_append(var, foo)" where
8359 * the assignment target is also an input to the RHS expression.
8360 *
8361 * Case 1 (RWOPT_TRANSFER rule): if the variable is "local" in the sense that
8362 * its declaration is not outside any BEGIN...EXCEPTION block surrounding the
8363 * assignment, then we do not need to worry about preserving its value if the
8364 * RHS expression throws an error.  If in addition the variable is referenced
8365 * exactly once in the RHS expression, then we can optimize by converting the
8366 * read/write expanded value into a transient value within the expression
8367 * evaluation context, and then setting the variable's recorded value to NULL
8368 * to prevent double-free attempts.  This works regardless of any other
8369 * details of the RHS expression.  If the expression eventually returns that
8370 * same expanded object (possibly modified) then the variable will re-acquire
8371 * ownership; while if it returns something else or throws an error, the
8372 * expanded object will be discarded as part of cleanup of the evaluation
8373 * context.
8374 *
8375 * Case 2 (RWOPT_INPLACE rule): if we have a non-local assignment or if
8376 * it looks like "var := array_append(var, var[1])" with multiple references
8377 * to the target variable, then we can't use case 1.  Nonetheless, if the
8378 * top-level function is trusted not to corrupt its argument in case of an
8379 * error, then when the var has an expanded object as value, it is safe to
8380 * pass the value as a read/write pointer to the top-level function and let
8381 * the function modify the value in-place.  (Any other references have to be
8382 * passed as read-only pointers as usual.)  Only the top-level function has to
8383 * be trusted, since if anything further down fails, the object hasn't been
8384 * modified yet.
8385 *
8386 * This function checks to see if the assignment is optimizable according
8387 * to either rule, and updates expr->expr_rwopt accordingly.  In addition,
8388 * it sets expr->expr_rw_param to the address of the Param within the
8389 * expression that can be passed as read/write (there can be only one);
8390 * or to NULL when there is no safe Param.
8391 *
8392 * Note that this mechanism intentionally allows just one Param to emit a
8393 * read/write pointer; in case 2, the expression could contain other Params
8394 * referencing the target variable, but those must be treated as read-only.
8395 *
8396 * Also note that we only apply this optimization within simple expressions.
8397 * There's no point in it for non-simple expressions, because the
8398 * exec_run_select code path will flatten any expanded result anyway.
8399 */
8400static void
8401exec_check_rw_parameter(PLpgSQL_expr *expr, int paramid)
8402{
8403	Expr	   *sexpr = expr->expr_simple_expr;
8404	Oid			funcid;
8405	List	   *fargs;
8406	Oid			prosupport;
8407
8408	/* Assume unsafe */
8409	expr->expr_rwopt = PLPGSQL_RWOPT_NOPE;
8410	expr->expr_rw_param = NULL;
8411
8412	/* Shouldn't be here for non-simple expression */
8413	Assert(sexpr != NULL);
8414
8415	/* Param should match the expression's assignment target, too */
8416	Assert(paramid == expr->target_param + 1);
8417
8418	/*
8419	 * If the assignment is to a "local" variable (one whose value won't
8420	 * matter anymore if expression evaluation fails), and this Param is the
8421	 * only reference to that variable in the expression, then we can
8422	 * unconditionally optimize using the "transfer" method.
8423	 */
8424	if (expr->target_is_local)
8425	{
8426		count_param_references_context context;
8427
8428		/* See how many references there are, and find one of them */
8429		context.paramid = paramid;
8430		context.count = 0;
8431		context.last_param = NULL;
8432		(void) count_param_references((Node *) sexpr, &context);
8433
8434		/* If we're here, the expr must contain some reference to the var */
8435		Assert(context.count > 0);
8436
8437		/* If exactly one reference, success! */
8438		if (context.count == 1)
8439		{
8440			expr->expr_rwopt = PLPGSQL_RWOPT_TRANSFER;
8441			expr->expr_rw_param = context.last_param;
8442			return;
8443		}
8444	}
8445
8446	/*
8447	 * Otherwise, see if we can trust the expression's top-level function to
8448	 * apply the "inplace" method.
8449	 *
8450	 * Top level of expression must be a simple FuncExpr, OpExpr, or
8451	 * SubscriptingRef, else we can't identify which function is relevant. But
8452	 * it's okay to look through any RelabelType above that, since that can't
8453	 * fail.
8454	 */
8455	if (IsA(sexpr, RelabelType))
8456		sexpr = ((RelabelType *) sexpr)->arg;
8457	if (IsA(sexpr, FuncExpr))
8458	{
8459		FuncExpr   *fexpr = (FuncExpr *) sexpr;
8460
8461		funcid = fexpr->funcid;
8462		fargs = fexpr->args;
8463	}
8464	else if (IsA(sexpr, OpExpr))
8465	{
8466		OpExpr	   *opexpr = (OpExpr *) sexpr;
8467
8468		funcid = opexpr->opfuncid;
8469		fargs = opexpr->args;
8470	}
8471	else if (IsA(sexpr, SubscriptingRef))
8472	{
8473		SubscriptingRef *sbsref = (SubscriptingRef *) sexpr;
8474
8475		funcid = get_typsubscript(sbsref->refcontainertype, NULL);
8476
8477		/*
8478		 * We assume that only the refexpr and refassgnexpr (if any) are
8479		 * relevant to the support function's decision.  If that turns out to
8480		 * be a bad idea, we could incorporate the subscript expressions into
8481		 * the fargs list somehow.
8482		 */
8483		fargs = list_make2(sbsref->refexpr, sbsref->refassgnexpr);
8484	}
8485	else
8486		return;
8487
8488	/*
8489	 * The top-level function must be one that can handle in-place update
8490	 * safely.  We allow functions to declare their ability to do that via a
8491	 * support function request.
8492	 */
8493	prosupport = get_func_support(funcid);
8494	if (OidIsValid(prosupport))
8495	{
8496		SupportRequestModifyInPlace req;
8497		Param	   *param;
8498
8499		req.type = T_SupportRequestModifyInPlace;
8500		req.funcid = funcid;
8501		req.args = fargs;
8502		req.paramid = paramid;
8503
8504		param = (Param *)
8505			DatumGetPointer(OidFunctionCall1(prosupport,
8506											 PointerGetDatum(&req)));
8507
8508		if (param == NULL)
8509			return;				/* support function fails */
8510
8511		/* Verify support function followed the API */
8512		Assert(IsA(param, Param));
8513		Assert(param->paramkind == PARAM_EXTERN);
8514		Assert(param->paramid == paramid);
8515
8516		/* Found the Param we want to pass as read/write */
8517		expr->expr_rwopt = PLPGSQL_RWOPT_INPLACE;
8518		expr->expr_rw_param = param;
8519		return;
8520	}
8521}
8522
8523/*
8524 * Count Params referencing the specified paramid, and return one of them
8525 * if there are any.
8526 *
8527 * We actually only need to distinguish 0, 1, and N references; so we can
8528 * abort the tree traversal as soon as we've found two.
8529 */
8530static bool
8531count_param_references(Node *node, count_param_references_context *context)
8532{
8533	if (node == NULL)
8534		return false;
8535	else if (IsA(node, Param))
8536	{
8537		Param	   *param = (Param *) node;
8538
8539		if (param->paramkind == PARAM_EXTERN &&
8540			param->paramid == context->paramid)
8541		{
8542			context->last_param = param;
8543			if (++(context->count) > 1)
8544				return true;	/* abort tree traversal */
8545		}
8546		return false;
8547	}
8548	else
8549		return expression_tree_walker(node, count_param_references, context);
8550}
8551
8552/*
8553 * exec_check_assignable --- is it OK to assign to the indicated datum?
8554 *
8555 * This should match pl_gram.y's check_assignable().
8556 */
8557static void
8558exec_check_assignable(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, int dno)
8559{
8560	PLpgSQL_datum *datum;
8561
8562	Assert(dno >= 0 && dno < estate->ndatums);
8563	datum = estate->datums[dno];
8564	switch (datum->dtype)
8565	{
8566		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR:
8567		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_PROMISE:
8568		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC:
8569			if (((PLpgSQL_variable *) datum)->isconst)
8570				ereport(ERROR,
8571						(errcode(ERRCODE_ERROR_IN_ASSIGNMENT),
8572						 errmsg("variable \"%s\" is declared CONSTANT",
8573								((PLpgSQL_variable *) datum)->refname)));
8574			break;
8575		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_ROW:
8576			/* always assignable; member vars were checked at compile time */
8577			break;
8578		case PLPGSQL_DTYPE_RECFIELD:
8579			/* assignable if parent record is */
8580			exec_check_assignable(estate,
8581								  ((PLpgSQL_recfield *) datum)->recparentno);
8582			break;
8583		default:
8584			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized dtype: %d", datum->dtype);
8585			break;
8586	}
8587}
8588
8589/* ----------
8590 * exec_set_found			Set the global found variable to true/false
8591 * ----------
8592 */
8593static void
8594exec_set_found(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, bool state)
8595{
8596	PLpgSQL_var *var;
8597
8598	var = (PLpgSQL_var *) (estate->datums[estate->found_varno]);
8599	assign_simple_var(estate, var, BoolGetDatum(state), false, false);
8600}
8601
8602/*
8603 * plpgsql_create_econtext --- create an eval_econtext for the current function
8604 *
8605 * We may need to create a new shared_simple_eval_estate too, if there's not
8606 * one already for the current transaction.  The EState will be cleaned up at
8607 * transaction end.  Ditto for shared_simple_eval_resowner.
8608 */
8609static void
8610plpgsql_create_econtext(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate)
8611{
8612	SimpleEcontextStackEntry *entry;
8613
8614	/*
8615	 * Create an EState for evaluation of simple expressions, if there's not
8616	 * one already in the current transaction.  The EState is made a child of
8617	 * TopTransactionContext so it will have the right lifespan.
8618	 *
8619	 * Note that this path is never taken when beginning a DO block; the
8620	 * required EState was already made by plpgsql_inline_handler.  However,
8621	 * if the DO block executes COMMIT or ROLLBACK, then we'll come here and
8622	 * make a shared EState to use for the rest of the DO block.  That's OK;
8623	 * see the comments for shared_simple_eval_estate.  (Note also that a DO
8624	 * block will continue to use its private cast hash table for the rest of
8625	 * the block.  That's okay for now, but it might cause problems someday.)
8626	 */
8627	if (estate->simple_eval_estate == NULL)
8628	{
8629		MemoryContext oldcontext;
8630
8631		if (shared_simple_eval_estate == NULL)
8632		{
8633			oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(TopTransactionContext);
8634			shared_simple_eval_estate = CreateExecutorState();
8635			MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
8636		}
8637		estate->simple_eval_estate = shared_simple_eval_estate;
8638	}
8639
8640	/*
8641	 * Likewise for the simple-expression resource owner.
8642	 */
8643	if (estate->simple_eval_resowner == NULL)
8644	{
8645		if (shared_simple_eval_resowner == NULL)
8646			shared_simple_eval_resowner =
8647				ResourceOwnerCreate(TopTransactionResourceOwner,
8648									"PL/pgSQL simple expressions");
8649		estate->simple_eval_resowner = shared_simple_eval_resowner;
8650	}
8651
8652	/*
8653	 * Create a child econtext for the current function.
8654	 */
8655	estate->eval_econtext = CreateExprContext(estate->simple_eval_estate);
8656
8657	/*
8658	 * Make a stack entry so we can clean up the econtext at subxact end.
8659	 * Stack entries are kept in TopTransactionContext for simplicity.
8660	 */
8661	entry = (SimpleEcontextStackEntry *)
8662		MemoryContextAlloc(TopTransactionContext,
8663						   sizeof(SimpleEcontextStackEntry));
8664
8665	entry->stack_econtext = estate->eval_econtext;
8666	entry->xact_subxid = GetCurrentSubTransactionId();
8667
8668	entry->next = simple_econtext_stack;
8669	simple_econtext_stack = entry;
8670}
8671
8672/*
8673 * plpgsql_destroy_econtext --- destroy function's econtext
8674 *
8675 * We check that it matches the top stack entry, and destroy the stack
8676 * entry along with the context.
8677 */
8678static void
8679plpgsql_destroy_econtext(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate)
8680{
8681	SimpleEcontextStackEntry *next;
8682
8683	Assert(simple_econtext_stack != NULL);
8684	Assert(simple_econtext_stack->stack_econtext == estate->eval_econtext);
8685
8686	next = simple_econtext_stack->next;
8687	pfree(simple_econtext_stack);
8688	simple_econtext_stack = next;
8689
8690	FreeExprContext(estate->eval_econtext, true);
8691	estate->eval_econtext = NULL;
8692}
8693
8694/*
8695 * plpgsql_xact_cb --- post-transaction-commit-or-abort cleanup
8696 *
8697 * If a simple-expression EState was created in the current transaction,
8698 * it has to be cleaned up.  The same for the simple-expression resowner.
8699 */
8700void
8701plpgsql_xact_cb(XactEvent event, void *arg)
8702{
8703	/*
8704	 * If we are doing a clean transaction shutdown, free the EState and tell
8705	 * the resowner to release whatever plancache references it has, so that
8706	 * all remaining resources will be released correctly.  (We don't need to
8707	 * actually delete the resowner here; deletion of the
8708	 * TopTransactionResourceOwner will take care of that.)
8709	 *
8710	 * In an abort, we expect the regular abort recovery procedures to release
8711	 * everything of interest, so just clear our pointers.
8712	 */
8713	if (event == XACT_EVENT_COMMIT ||
8714		event == XACT_EVENT_PARALLEL_COMMIT ||
8715		event == XACT_EVENT_PREPARE)
8716	{
8717		simple_econtext_stack = NULL;
8718
8719		if (shared_simple_eval_estate)
8720			FreeExecutorState(shared_simple_eval_estate);
8721		shared_simple_eval_estate = NULL;
8722		if (shared_simple_eval_resowner)
8723			ReleaseAllPlanCacheRefsInOwner(shared_simple_eval_resowner);
8724		shared_simple_eval_resowner = NULL;
8725	}
8726	else if (event == XACT_EVENT_ABORT ||
8727			 event == XACT_EVENT_PARALLEL_ABORT)
8728	{
8729		simple_econtext_stack = NULL;
8730		shared_simple_eval_estate = NULL;
8731		shared_simple_eval_resowner = NULL;
8732	}
8733}
8734
8735/*
8736 * plpgsql_subxact_cb --- post-subtransaction-commit-or-abort cleanup
8737 *
8738 * Make sure any simple-expression econtexts created in the current
8739 * subtransaction get cleaned up.  We have to do this explicitly because
8740 * no other code knows which econtexts belong to which level of subxact.
8741 */
8742void
8743plpgsql_subxact_cb(SubXactEvent event, SubTransactionId mySubid,
8744				   SubTransactionId parentSubid, void *arg)
8745{
8746	if (event == SUBXACT_EVENT_COMMIT_SUB || event == SUBXACT_EVENT_ABORT_SUB)
8747	{
8748		while (simple_econtext_stack != NULL &&
8749			   simple_econtext_stack->xact_subxid == mySubid)
8750		{
8751			SimpleEcontextStackEntry *next;
8752
8753			FreeExprContext(simple_econtext_stack->stack_econtext,
8754							(event == SUBXACT_EVENT_COMMIT_SUB));
8755			next = simple_econtext_stack->next;
8756			pfree(simple_econtext_stack);
8757			simple_econtext_stack = next;
8758		}
8759	}
8760}
8761
8762/*
8763 * assign_simple_var --- assign a new value to any VAR datum.
8764 *
8765 * This should be the only mechanism for assignment to simple variables,
8766 * lest we do the release of the old value incorrectly (not to mention
8767 * the detoasting business).
8768 */
8769static void
8770assign_simple_var(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, PLpgSQL_var *var,
8771				  Datum newvalue, bool isnull, bool freeable)
8772{
8773	Assert(var->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_VAR ||
8774		   var->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_PROMISE);
8775
8776	/*
8777	 * In non-atomic contexts, we do not want to store TOAST pointers in
8778	 * variables, because such pointers might become stale after a commit.
8779	 * Forcibly detoast in such cases.  We don't want to detoast (flatten)
8780	 * expanded objects, however; those should be OK across a transaction
8781	 * boundary since they're just memory-resident objects.  (Elsewhere in
8782	 * this module, operations on expanded records likewise need to request
8783	 * detoasting of record fields when !estate->atomic.  Expanded arrays are
8784	 * not a problem since all array entries are always detoasted.)
8785	 */
8786	if (!estate->atomic && !isnull && var->datatype->typlen == -1 &&
8787		VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL_NON_EXPANDED(DatumGetPointer(newvalue)))
8788	{
8789		MemoryContext oldcxt;
8790		Datum		detoasted;
8791
8792		/*
8793		 * Do the detoasting in the eval_mcontext to avoid long-term leakage
8794		 * of whatever memory toast fetching might leak.  Then we have to copy
8795		 * the detoasted datum to the function's main context, which is a
8796		 * pain, but there's little choice.
8797		 */
8798		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
8799		detoasted = PointerGetDatum(detoast_external_attr((struct varlena *) DatumGetPointer(newvalue)));
8800		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
8801		/* Now's a good time to not leak the input value if it's freeable */
8802		if (freeable)
8803			pfree(DatumGetPointer(newvalue));
8804		/* Once we copy the value, it's definitely freeable */
8805		newvalue = datumCopy(detoasted, false, -1);
8806		freeable = true;
8807		/* Can't clean up eval_mcontext here, but it'll happen before long */
8808	}
8809
8810	/* Free the old value if needed */
8811	if (var->freeval)
8812	{
8813		if (DatumIsReadWriteExpandedObject(var->value,
8814										   var->isnull,
8815										   var->datatype->typlen))
8816			DeleteExpandedObject(var->value);
8817		else
8818			pfree(DatumGetPointer(var->value));
8819	}
8820	/* Assign new value to datum */
8821	var->value = newvalue;
8822	var->isnull = isnull;
8823	var->freeval = freeable;
8824
8825	/*
8826	 * If it's a promise variable, then either we just assigned the promised
8827	 * value, or the user explicitly assigned an overriding value.  Either
8828	 * way, cancel the promise.
8829	 */
8830	var->promise = PLPGSQL_PROMISE_NONE;
8831}
8832
8833/*
8834 * free old value of a text variable and assign new value from C string
8835 */
8836static void
8837assign_text_var(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, PLpgSQL_var *var, const char *str)
8838{
8839	assign_simple_var(estate, var, CStringGetTextDatum(str), false, true);
8840}
8841
8842/*
8843 * assign_record_var --- assign a new value to any REC datum.
8844 */
8845static void
8846assign_record_var(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, PLpgSQL_rec *rec,
8847				  ExpandedRecordHeader *erh)
8848{
8849	Assert(rec->dtype == PLPGSQL_DTYPE_REC);
8850
8851	/* Transfer new record object into datum_context */
8852	TransferExpandedRecord(erh, estate->datum_context);
8853
8854	/* Free the old value ... */
8855	if (rec->erh)
8856		DeleteExpandedObject(ExpandedRecordGetDatum(rec->erh));
8857
8858	/* ... and install the new */
8859	rec->erh = erh;
8860}
8861
8862/*
8863 * exec_eval_using_params --- evaluate params of USING clause
8864 *
8865 * The result data structure is created in the stmt_mcontext, and should
8866 * be freed by resetting that context.
8867 */
8868static ParamListInfo
8869exec_eval_using_params(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate, List *params)
8870{
8871	ParamListInfo paramLI;
8872	int			nargs;
8873	MemoryContext stmt_mcontext;
8874	MemoryContext oldcontext;
8875	int			i;
8876	ListCell   *lc;
8877
8878	/* Fast path for no parameters: we can just return NULL */
8879	if (params == NIL)
8880		return NULL;
8881
8882	nargs = list_length(params);
8883	stmt_mcontext = get_stmt_mcontext(estate);
8884	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(stmt_mcontext);
8885	paramLI = makeParamList(nargs);
8886	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
8887
8888	i = 0;
8889	foreach(lc, params)
8890	{
8891		PLpgSQL_expr *param = (PLpgSQL_expr *) lfirst(lc);
8892		ParamExternData *prm = &paramLI->params[i];
8893		int32		ppdtypmod;
8894
8895		/*
8896		 * Always mark params as const, since we only use the result with
8897		 * one-shot plans.
8898		 */
8899		prm->pflags = PARAM_FLAG_CONST;
8900
8901		prm->value = exec_eval_expr(estate, param,
8902									&prm->isnull,
8903									&prm->ptype,
8904									&ppdtypmod);
8905
8906		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(stmt_mcontext);
8907
8908		if (prm->ptype == UNKNOWNOID)
8909		{
8910			/*
8911			 * Treat 'unknown' parameters as text, since that's what most
8912			 * people would expect.  The SPI functions can coerce unknown
8913			 * constants in a more intelligent way, but not unknown Params.
8914			 * This code also takes care of copying into the right context.
8915			 * Note we assume 'unknown' has the representation of C-string.
8916			 */
8917			prm->ptype = TEXTOID;
8918			if (!prm->isnull)
8919				prm->value = CStringGetTextDatum(DatumGetCString(prm->value));
8920		}
8921		/* pass-by-ref non null values must be copied into stmt_mcontext */
8922		else if (!prm->isnull)
8923		{
8924			int16		typLen;
8925			bool		typByVal;
8926
8927			get_typlenbyval(prm->ptype, &typLen, &typByVal);
8928			if (!typByVal)
8929				prm->value = datumCopy(prm->value, typByVal, typLen);
8930		}
8931
8932		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
8933
8934		exec_eval_cleanup(estate);
8935
8936		i++;
8937	}
8938
8939	return paramLI;
8940}
8941
8942/*
8943 * Open portal for dynamic query
8944 *
8945 * Caution: this resets the stmt_mcontext at exit.  We might eventually need
8946 * to move that responsibility to the callers, but currently no caller needs
8947 * to have statement-lifetime temp data that survives past this, so it's
8948 * simpler to do it here.
8949 */
8950static Portal
8951exec_dynquery_with_params(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
8952						  PLpgSQL_expr *dynquery,
8953						  List *params,
8954						  const char *portalname,
8955						  int cursorOptions)
8956{
8957	Portal		portal;
8958	Datum		query;
8959	bool		isnull;
8960	Oid			restype;
8961	int32		restypmod;
8962	char	   *querystr;
8963	SPIParseOpenOptions options;
8964	MemoryContext stmt_mcontext = get_stmt_mcontext(estate);
8965
8966	/*
8967	 * Evaluate the string expression after the EXECUTE keyword. Its result is
8968	 * the querystring we have to execute.
8969	 */
8970	query = exec_eval_expr(estate, dynquery, &isnull, &restype, &restypmod);
8971	if (isnull)
8972		ereport(ERROR,
8973				(errcode(ERRCODE_NULL_VALUE_NOT_ALLOWED),
8974				 errmsg("query string argument of EXECUTE is null")));
8975
8976	/* Get the C-String representation */
8977	querystr = convert_value_to_string(estate, query, restype);
8978
8979	/* copy it into the stmt_mcontext before we clean up */
8980	querystr = MemoryContextStrdup(stmt_mcontext, querystr);
8981
8982	exec_eval_cleanup(estate);
8983
8984	/*
8985	 * Open an implicit cursor for the query.  We use SPI_cursor_parse_open
8986	 * even when there are no params, because this avoids making and freeing
8987	 * one copy of the plan.
8988	 */
8989	memset(&options, 0, sizeof(options));
8990	options.params = exec_eval_using_params(estate, params);
8991	options.cursorOptions = cursorOptions;
8992	options.read_only = estate->readonly_func;
8993
8994	portal = SPI_cursor_parse_open(portalname, querystr, &options);
8995
8996	if (portal == NULL)
8997		elog(ERROR, "could not open implicit cursor for query \"%s\": %s",
8998			 querystr, SPI_result_code_string(SPI_result));
8999
9000	/* Release transient data */
9001	MemoryContextReset(stmt_mcontext);
9002
9003	return portal;
9004}
9005
9006/*
9007 * Return a formatted string with information about an expression's parameters,
9008 * or NULL if the expression does not take any parameters.
9009 * The result is in the eval_mcontext.
9010 */
9011static char *
9012format_expr_params(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
9013				   const PLpgSQL_expr *expr)
9014{
9015	int			paramno;
9016	int			dno;
9017	StringInfoData paramstr;
9018	MemoryContext oldcontext;
9019
9020	if (!expr->paramnos)
9021		return NULL;
9022
9023	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
9024
9025	initStringInfo(&paramstr);
9026	paramno = 0;
9027	dno = -1;
9028	while ((dno = bms_next_member(expr->paramnos, dno)) >= 0)
9029	{
9030		Datum		paramdatum;
9031		Oid			paramtypeid;
9032		bool		paramisnull;
9033		int32		paramtypmod;
9034		PLpgSQL_var *curvar;
9035
9036		curvar = (PLpgSQL_var *) estate->datums[dno];
9037
9038		exec_eval_datum(estate, (PLpgSQL_datum *) curvar,
9039						&paramtypeid, &paramtypmod,
9040						&paramdatum, &paramisnull);
9041
9042		appendStringInfo(&paramstr, "%s%s = ",
9043						 paramno > 0 ? ", " : "",
9044						 curvar->refname);
9045
9046		if (paramisnull)
9047			appendStringInfoString(&paramstr, "NULL");
9048		else
9049			appendStringInfoStringQuoted(&paramstr,
9050										 convert_value_to_string(estate,
9051																 paramdatum,
9052																 paramtypeid),
9053										 -1);
9054
9055		paramno++;
9056	}
9057
9058	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
9059
9060	return paramstr.data;
9061}
9062
9063/*
9064 * Return a formatted string with information about the parameter values,
9065 * or NULL if there are no parameters.
9066 * The result is in the eval_mcontext.
9067 */
9068static char *
9069format_preparedparamsdata(PLpgSQL_execstate *estate,
9070						  ParamListInfo paramLI)
9071{
9072	int			paramno;
9073	StringInfoData paramstr;
9074	MemoryContext oldcontext;
9075
9076	if (!paramLI)
9077		return NULL;
9078
9079	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(get_eval_mcontext(estate));
9080
9081	initStringInfo(&paramstr);
9082	for (paramno = 0; paramno < paramLI->numParams; paramno++)
9083	{
9084		ParamExternData *prm = &paramLI->params[paramno];
9085
9086		/*
9087		 * Note: for now, this is only used on ParamListInfos produced by
9088		 * exec_eval_using_params(), so we don't worry about invoking the
9089		 * paramFetch hook or skipping unused parameters.
9090		 */
9091		appendStringInfo(&paramstr, "%s$%d = ",
9092						 paramno > 0 ? ", " : "",
9093						 paramno + 1);
9094
9095		if (prm->isnull)
9096			appendStringInfoString(&paramstr, "NULL");
9097		else
9098			appendStringInfoStringQuoted(&paramstr,
9099										 convert_value_to_string(estate,
9100																 prm->value,
9101																 prm->ptype),
9102										 -1);
9103	}
9104
9105	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
9106
9107	return paramstr.data;
9108}
